findings.description: Committed evidence sample generated from dirty tree at RED commit (git_dirty_flag:true, commit=c94640c); no check enforces clean/HEAD-equal at generation time; binding-class exclusion lets it pass all tests. Repudiation failure per STRIDE/TB-004.
findings.instance_fix: Regenerate sample from clean GREEN checkout; regen-sample.sh refuses dirty tree.
findings.class_fix: Unit test asserts committed sample's git_dirty_flag==false and commit is ancestor of HEAD.
findings.fix_note: FIXED: regen-sample.sh refuses a dirty tree (exit 40, ignoring only untracked out/ run products) and QA001_CommittedSamples_CleanTreeAncestorCommit asserts git_dirty_flag==false + 40-hex commit + ancestor-of-HEAD. Sample regeneration itself is the orchestrator step (clean tree). FOLLOW-UP (fix round 1b): regen sequencing corrected — both reports stay under out/ until BOTH runs complete, then the pair is published together; publishing the variance sample before the canonical run had dirtied the tree and baked git_dirty_flag:true into the canonical sample.

findings.description: executed_solver_sha256/solver_path_concrete record the retained release ARCHIVE, not the passed solver path + its digest; README/ADR claim otherwise (docs lie); P04 never re-verifies installed binary against provisioning's binary.sha256 — post-provisioning substitution passes P04/P05/P06/P07.
findings.instance_fix: Record passed path + Sha256File(path) as executed_solver_sha256; separate solver_archive_sha256 field; P04 compares installed binary digest vs provisioning record; correct README+ADR.
findings.class_fix: RS-016 anti-hardcode test asserts executed_solver_sha256 equals recomputed digest of file at the option-manifest solver path.
findings.fix_note: FIXED: executed_solver_sha256 recomputed at emission from the file at the option-manifest solver path (binary); solver_archive_sha256 separate field carrying the BND-002 archive pin; P04 compares the installed binary digest against provisioning binary.sha256; README+ADR corrected; Inv004/Inv009 anti-hardcode assert executed==digest-of-binary-path and executed!=archive.

findings.description: Hardcoded class-2 evidence: decoy_invocations=0 and decoys_present=true emitted as literals in BuildDeterministic; failed decoy plant (IOException swallowed) still reports decoys present.
findings.instance_fix: Populate from actual decoy log count and post-plant existence check; INCOMPLETE if planting failed.
findings.class_fix: Induced-failure test plants pre-invoked decoy log and asserts emitted decoy_invocations nonzero.
findings.fix_note: FIXED: decoy_invocations from the actually-read decoy log; decoys_present from a post-plant existence check; planting failure returns startup INCOMPLETE (no swallowed IOException); QA003 induced test plants a pre-invoked log and asserts nonzero.

findings.description: P03 universe silently narrowed to Dafny*/Boogie*/System.CommandLine during GREEN via oracle note + matching test filter; other package assemblies loaded via .deps.json (System.Reactive, LanguageServer deps, Newtonsoft.Json) never hash-compared — substitution invisible.
findings.instance_fix: Widen capture to all non-framework assemblies selected through the route's .deps.json (codex F8 universe) and re-capture expected sets — or take narrowing through spec change.
findings.class_fix: Machine-readable universe predicate in the oracle file consumed by BOTH harness and test; narrowing becomes reviewable oracle diff.
findings.fix_note: FIXED: universe widened to the codex-F8 definition (every non-framework .deps.json package runtime asset) via a machine-readable universe_predicate in the oracle consumed by BOTH harness (PackageRuntimeAssets) and test; expected-loaded sets re-captured (23 route A, 21 route B); Inv002 P03 test set-equality over the full universe with a >14 and non-Dafny/Boogie assertion.

findings.description: Typed causes lie: write_failed_report hardcodes cause=WallClockExpiry reused for 5 non-timeout failure classes; malformed route report collapses to missing-report variant (spec requires own variant); run-level unadjudicated-failure variant unreachable; aggregator writes adjudication_records:null unconditionally.
findings.instance_fix: Parameterize cause per fail_run site; MalformedReport-tagged RouteReport in aggregator catch; propagate route adjudication_records and map to unadjudicated-failure variant.
findings.class_fix: One induced-failure test per committed verdict_reason_union variant asserting variant AND cause match the induced fault class.
findings.fix_note: FIXED: write_failed_report/fail_run parameterized per-site cause; aggregator tags malformed route reports with the malformed-report variant; route adjudication_records propagate to the run level and map to the unadjudicated-failure variant; QA005 tests assert prerequisite cause != WallClockExpiry and malformed-report own variant.

findings.description: Spec-composition catch-22: every in-suite fresh run is variance-mode (recursion guard) and final_suite_status/route_verdicts are class-2, so a canonical COMPATIBLE-bearing sample can never be committed without failing fresh-run-equality; ADR-0001 stuck at pending; Phase 0.1 gate unsatisfiable without spec decision.
findings.instance_fix: Spec decision required: (a) second canonical-run committed sample with schema-declared suite-status mask, or (b) reclassify final_suite_status comparison via spec change.
findings.class_fix: N/A — inherently spec-level conflict.
findings.fix_note: FIXED (spec-amended): schema v2 declares suite_status_mask (additive-only); committed sample PAIR — variance (full equality) + canonical (masked equality via schema-declared fields); DeterministicProjection(applySuiteStatusMask) drops only the schema-declared subtree; fresh-run-equality checks both; regen-sample.sh regenerates the pair; ADR cites the canonical sample. FOLLOW-UP (fix round 1b): the canonical leg of regen-sample.sh now passes NO overrides (a --out flag was silently flipping run-spike.sh to variance mode, skipping the suite); the report is recovered from the controller completion line, a canonicity self-check refuses to publish a sample with final_suite_status=unknown, and the documented two-pass bootstrap convergence (first pass records suite failure against stale committed samples; commit, re-run once for the citable success-status sample) is detected and reported by the script.

findings.description: Per-phase setsid supervision documented but not implemented: one setsid session for the whole inner controller; config declares per_phase_process_groups:setsid and a test green-checks the string (AP-011 certifying AP-004 dead machinery). Detached children escape TERM->KILL sweep.
findings.instance_fix: Per-phase setsid groups with supervisor tracking per-phase pgids + induced hang test for a non-probe phase — or amend spec/config/test to single-session design explicitly.
findings.class_fix: Every supervision config key must pair with a behavioral induced-failure test (extend RS-012 subset); ban config-string-only enforcement.
findings.fix_note: FIXED: run_with_env launches every phase in its own setsid -w session; supervise_phase tracks the per-phase session leader and enforces the single /proc/uptime deadline with TERM->KILL; phase_guard maps 124 to WallClockExpiry per phase; QA007 test plants a slow build target (inject.targets imported by Directory.Build.targets in the test run root) and asserts the supervisor kills the NON-probe build phase.

findings.description: Controller exits 0 even on suite failure/probe failure/INCOMPLETE (SUITE_EXIT/AGG_EXIT captured, never propagated); exit channel fail-open for CI wiring while report/summary are fail-closed.
findings.instance_fix: Exit nonzero on SUITE_STATUS=failure or aggregator nonzero; document controller exit codes in README.
findings.class_fix: Induced-failure tests assert controller exit code nonzero.
findings.fix_note: FIXED: controller computes CONTROLLER_EXIT nonzero on suite failure, aggregator nonzero, or any route child nonzero; README documents controller exit codes; QA008 test asserts nonzero on child failure.

findings.description: P01 per-route partition cosmetic at restore level: single solution-wide restore exit stamped onto both P01(A) and P01(B); Route B lock failure still vetoes Route A (fail-closed direction, but exactly the R4-07 contradiction). Non-veto test exercises aggregation algebra only.
findings.instance_fix: Per-partition restores (or per-project lock validation) deriving P01(A)/P01(B) independently.
findings.class_fix: Induced-failure subset must include a route-scoped fault (mutate only Route B's lock in scratch) asserting the other route unaffected end-to-end.
findings.fix_note: FIXED: P01 derived per partition from independent per-project locked restores (restore-receipt p01_partitions.*.exit); aggregator stamps P01(A)/P01(B) from those; QA009 test mutates ONLY Route B lock in scratch and asserts Route A restore unaffected while Route B fails. Run still fails closed overall (documented) but attribution is non-vetoing.

findings.description: GREEN edited committed oracles outside the sanctioned TEST_BUG list: ok.sidecar min_task_count 3->2 (documented AP-014 recapture, plausible but unadjudicated), syntax-error.dfy planted token moved (P08 location oracle redefined), expected-loaded universe note (see QA-004). tdd-test-edits.log absent; audit reconstructed from git.
findings.instance_fix: Record the three oracle edits with adjudication rationale in workflow artifacts; independently confirm min_task_count=2 for Dafny 4.11.
findings.class_fix: Treat fixtures/, fixtures/expected/, manifest/ as test-owned in the GREEN edit contract; orchestrator diffs those paths RED->GREEN.
findings.fix_note: FIXED: three oracle edits adjudicated here — (1) ok.sidecar min_task_count 3->2: Dafny 4.11 yields 3 verification targets for ok.dfy but only 2 solver tasks; the trivial function Add has NO proof obligation, so 2 (>0) is the correct non-vacuity floor (independently confirmed by QA010_OkFixture test asserting 3 targets / 2 tasks / missing=Add). (2) syntax-error.dfy planted-token line normalization: the parse error is now on the committed expected line 4 (method Broken( { ). (3) expected-loaded universe note reversed by QA-004 (widened to codex-F8). tdd-test-edits.log was absent; this note is the adjudication record. Class fix: fixtures/, fixtures/expected/, manifest/ are test-owned oracle paths — the orchestrator diffs them RED->GREEN.

findings.description: Flake candidate (1-in-10 gate failure): abandoned P05 sentinel leg (Task.Wait(45s) timeout leaves Boogie running) can invoke the sentinel stub during P08/P09's before/after ledger window, spuriously failing them inside nested controller runs used by no-args Facts (RunTwice, CommittedSample equality, ForgedExtraReport) — consistent with observed evidence. Unconfirmed without lost TRX.
findings.instance_fix: Refuse to start P08/P09 while a prior sentinel leg is unsettled (join/kill prover children), or per-probe sub-nonce in stub argv so P08/P09 count only their own window.
findings.class_fix: Nonce-scope every ledger-delta assertion per probe, not per run; reproduce by forcing P05 timeout branch in a loop.
findings.fix_note: FIXED: per-probe sub-nonce in the sentinel stub argv (probe:<tag>); ReadLedger(probeTag) counts only that probe window; P08/P09/P05 snapshot before/after per their own tag and JoinPendingSentinelLeg bounds an abandoned prior leg; QA011 test plants a foreign-sub-nonce entry under the run nonce and proves P05 counts only its own.

findings.description: Startup gate checks only FIRST exact-name z3 on PATH, recognizes decoys by spoofable content marker, never examines deeper PATH entries or versioned names (z3-4.12.1) on PATH; weaker than EA-005 text. Exploitation requires option-wiring breakage P05 would catch — hence UNCERTAIN.
findings.instance_fix: Scan every PATH dir for z3*-named executables; recognize sanctioned decoys by exact digest.
findings.class_fix: Source-verify and cite Dafny 4.11's ambient-discovery probe sequence; generate the gate's check list from it.
findings.fix_note: FIXED: the gate scans EVERY PATH dir for z3*-named executables; sanctioned recording decoys recognized by exact script digest (harness-planted, cross-root) or by sanctioned decoy LOCATION (decoys/ dir or assembly-adjacent fallback); check list source-cited to Dafny 4.11 DafnyOptions.cs:1206-1227; QA012 test plants an unsanctioned first-on-PATH z3 and asserts the gate fails closed.

findings.description: Dev-loop honesty gap: direct dotnet test consumes last controller run's runsettings; uncommitted source edits leave HEAD unchanged so suite validates stale binaries (canonical controller run immune).
findings.instance_fix: Controller records source-set content digest in run-context.json; cheap test compares vs current tree, fails with re-run message.
findings.class_fix: Every run-context consumer asserts substrate identity (AP-003 digest-match rule).
findings.fix_note: FIXED: the controller records source_set_sha256 + a .source-manifest (git ls-files digests) in run-context; QA013 test asserts the manifest per-file digests equal the current tree (AP-003 substrate match) and fails with a re-run message on drift.

findings.description: Hygiene grab-bag: provision temp dirs/.corrupt files accumulate; watchdog PID-file race leaks inner session; P06 solver-unavailable classified by message-text sniff ('Z3 not found'); 26 antipattern-scan hits adjudicated benign (22 legitimate operator output, 4 error-suppressions safe).
findings.instance_fix: rm extract dirs/.corrupt after use; PID file before slow work + pgid fallback; typed prover-startup check instead of message sniff.
findings.class_fix: N/A — individually reviewed items; no shared structural cause.
findings.fix_note: FIXED: provision removes extract dirs and .corrupt/.download stragglers; controller cleans junk post-aggregation; PID file written before slow work with a session-leader fallback in the watchdog (QA-014(2)); P06 solver-unavailable is now a typed prover-startup check (z3 -version), not a message-text sniff.

findings.description: QA-012 treatment partially regressed by dead code: HarnessCore.IsSanctionedDecoyByDigest returns true for ANY z3 in ANY directory named 'decoys' — the exact-digest comparison above it is unreachable as a gate (both branches return true), so sanctioning is by spoofable LOCATION NAME, weaker than the round-1 fix claim ('recognized by exact script digest') and weaker than the content-marker it replaced for that location class. A NON-recording (real) z3 planted in a decoys/-named dir first on PATH passes the startup gate AND blinds the zero-decoy-invocation behavioral backstop (a real z3 logs nothing). Exploitation still requires broken solver-path wiring (P05 catches), matching round-1 UNCERTAIN posture; the honesty defect is that the code comment and fix_note claim digest recognition the code does not enforce.
findings.instance_fix: In IsSanctionedDecoyByDigest, make the decoys/-dir branch return the digest-comparison result (delete the unconditional 'return true' fallthrough); tests that plant custom recording decoys should plant them at the assembly-adjacent sanctioned path or pass their run root so the expected script matches.
findings.class_fix: Startup-gate sanctioning rules must be single-predicate (digest) with a test planting a NON-matching file at each sanctioned location and asserting gate failure.
findings.fix_note: FIXED: IsSanctionedDecoyByDigest's decoys/-dir branch now RETURNS the digest comparison (unconditional 'return true' deleted) — a decoys/-named dir sanctions nothing by location. HarnessCore.SanctionedDecoyScript(runRoot) exposes the exact script so tests plant digest-matching decoys; the Inv003 Decoys test's first-on-PATH decoy is now the sanctioned script (observable: the run root's decoy-invocations log), its custom recorder stays at the assembly-adjacent location. New QA015 test plants a wrong-digest z3 in a decoys/ dir first on PATH and asserts the gate fails closed (on reversion the run passes, so the test discriminates). Residual (per instance_fix design): the assembly-adjacent location remains location-sanctioned with the zero-invocation decoy-log assertion as behavioral backstop — recorded in ADR-0001 residuals.

findings.description: QA-008 residual fail-open window: SpikeAggregator now returns ExitCodes.RouteProbesPassed UNCONDITIONALLY (Program.cs final return; 'aggregation success' semantics), and run-spike.sh derives CONTROLLER_EXIT only from SUITE_STATUS/AGG_EXIT/child exits. Failures DETECTED AT AGGREGATION (P04 post-suite solver substitution, P02 build-receipt mismatch, missing/malformed route report with child exit 0, run_id-forged report rejection) yield a non-COMPATIBLE run report with controller exit 0 in a canonical run whose suite passed — a failure report behind a success exit on the CI-facing channel QA-008 named. Aggregator crashes/schema-anchor failures do exit nonzero (top-level exception).
findings.instance_fix: Controller (or aggregator) must also gate the exit on the emitted run report: nonzero when exit_report_matrix_outcome != 'consistent' or any per-probe result failed; variance runs with all probes passing still exit 0 so nested in-suite launches keep working.
findings.class_fix: Every exit-channel claim needs an induced-failure test per failure CLASS (child, suite, aggregation-computed, aggregator-crash); QA008 test covers only the child class.
findings.fix_note: FIXED: SpikeAggregator's final exit derives from the EMITTED report — 10 on any per-probe fail; 20 on any incomplete entry, exit/report-matrix inconsistency, or any route verdict that is neither COMPATIBLE nor the variance-run suite-failure INCOMPLETE (the last clause catches forged-run_id rejections whose probes still enter the all-pass combined view); 0 only for all-pass consistent aggregation, so nested variance launches keep exit 0. Controller exit already keys on AGG_EXIT, closing the CI channel. Class tests per failure class: child (QA008, round 1), aggregation-computed (new QA016 missing-report / forged-run_id / malformed tests, each asserting fail-closed exit + right-reason verdict variant, plus an all-pass exit-0 positive control), aggregator-crash (Inv009 schema-ordering test, round 0). Residual: the suite-failure class exit (SUITE_STATUS=failure -> controller 1) is exercised only by real canonical runs (regen bootstrap pass-1) — inducing a failing suite in-suite would recurse; documented here. README documents the aggregator exit contract; the exit/report matrix in the schema is unchanged (route-CHILD semantics).

findings.description: QA-007 treatment incomplete for two named phases: the provision phase (run-spike.sh: foreground 'run_cmd bash -p provision-z3.sh', ~line 641) and the BND-004 net8 control-runtime curl/tar (~lines 766-780) do NOT run in their own setsid session under supervise_phase — a hang there blocks the inner controller (which then cannot check any clock, the exact codex R4-05 concern) and is caught only by the OUTER watchdog at DEADLINE+20s with a generic WallClockExpiry cause, not per-phase attribution. Spec INV-006 says EVERY phase runs in its own setsid group and provisioning hangs are 'independently killable and independently tested'; the QA007 induced-hang test covers only the build phase.
findings.instance_fix: Route provision and the BND-004 fetch through run_with_env (new 'provision'/'fetch' launch classes) so they get per-phase setsid + TERM->KILL + phase_guard attribution; add an induced provision-hang test (e.g. curl to a test-owned stalling endpoint or a planted slow provision script in a scratch root).
findings.class_fix: The RS-012 induced-failure subset must include one hang per PHASE named in INV-006, not one representative phase.
findings.fix_note: FIXED: provision runs via run_with_env under new launch class 'provision' (SPIKE_RID_OVERRIDE fault hook explicitly passed through so the INV-014 RID test still reaches it); the BND-004 curl fetch AND the control-runtime tar extraction run under new class 'fetch' — all three get per-phase setsid sessions, TERM->KILL at the single /proc/uptime deadline, and typed phase_guard/fail_run attribution (fetch/extraction failures now also produce typed PrerequisiteFailure synthetics instead of a bare set -e abort). Induced hang: QA017 test plants a 400GB SPARSE file at the run root's staged-archive path — provisioning's digest check (sha256sum) genuinely blocks past a 30s bound with no network dependence — and asserts the supervisor kill, the PROVISION-phase attribution in the synthetic report, the [bound, bound+grace] duration window, and (QA-018 class fix) that no process of the killed session survives.

findings.description: Orphaned-process-group paths in the QA-007 supervision design: (a) pgid-file race — if the deadline fires between mktemp and the phase leader writing its pid, supervise_phase kills only the wrapper; the detached setsid phase session survives, and since the inner controller then exits promptly (fail_run 20), the outer watchdog never sweeps — the hung phase (e.g. dotnet/Boogie/Z3 tree) runs unbounded; (b) nested controller runs launched by the suite phase setsid their OWN inner sessions, which are NOT in the test phase's process group — TERM/KILL of the test-phase pgid (or of the outer sweep's inner-session group) orphans nested inner controllers carrying fresh 1800s deadlines of their own; they keep running for up to 30 minutes, mutating out/ (including out/current/spike.runsettings) after the parent run is dead.
findings.instance_fix: (a) have supervise_phase, when pgidfile is empty at deadline, poll briefly for the pid then fall back to find_session_leader(wrapper) before giving up; (b) record nested-run session leaders (or pass an inherited deadline cap: nested SPIKE_DEADLINE = min(own bound, parent remaining)) so a killed parent cannot leave longer-lived children.
findings.class_fix: Every supervisor kill path needs a test asserting no process of the killed phase's session survives (sweep /proc by session id after the induced kill).
findings.fix_note: FIXED: (a) supervise_phase, on an empty pgidfile at deadline, polls up to 5s for the phase leader's pid and then falls back to find_session_leader(wrapper) before killing — the detached phase session can no longer escape the sweep on the mktemp->write race. (b) Inherited deadline cap: the controller injects SPIKE_PARENT_DEADLINE (its absolute /proc/uptime deadline) into the suite-phase environment; the test launcher (Launch.ScriptCore) propagates it to nested controller launches unless a test overrides; the outer watchdog computes DEADLINE = min(own bound, parent deadline) — a killed parent can never leave nested inner controllers on fresh 30-min budgets (orphan lifetime bounded by the parent's own deadline + grace). Tests: QA018 cap test launches a nested run with a ~6s parent deadline against the committed 1800s config and asserts the wall-clock INCOMPLETE within the window (on reversion the healthy run exits 0); the /proc survivor sweep runs after the QA017 induced kill (class fix). The (a) race itself is not deterministically inducible — the fallback path is the treatment; the sweep asserts the invariant it protects.

findings.description: QA-006 residual: the canonical sample's masked subtree (final_suite_status, route_verdicts, verdict_reasons) — the exact fields ADR-0001 verdict citations will use — is verified by NOTHING in the suite: committed samples are never run through EvidenceSchema.ValidateReport, no test recomputes the canonical sample's route verdicts from its own per_probe_results + final_suite_status via VerdictAggregator, and no test asserts the committed canonical sample carries final_suite_status='success' (a half-done bootstrap pass-1 pair, suite=failure, is committable and keeps the whole suite green). Only regen-sample.sh provenance plus human diff review guard these fields (DD-008 sanctions the review, but round-1 QA-001 treated a comparable provenance-only gap as a repudiation finding). Forward guard exists only at promotion time (AdrLinter requires adjudication records for positive claims).
findings.instance_fix: Add a committed-sample integrity test: ValidateReport on both samples; recompute route verdicts from the canonical sample's own per_probe_results+final_suite_status via the aggregator formula and assert equality with its route_verdicts; assert the canonical sample's final_suite_status is 'success' whenever the ADR's adr_lint block carries any non-pending verdict.
findings.class_fix: Any schema-declared equality mask must pair with an internal-consistency check over the masked fields — masked never means unvalidated.
findings.fix_note: FIXED (no sample regeneration; tests pass against the committed pair as-is): (1) QA019 schema test runs BOTH committed samples through EvidenceSchema.ValidateReport (closed schema) after the trust-anchor check. (2) QA019 recompute test rebuilds each sample's route verdicts from its OWN per_probe_results + final_suite_status through VerdictAggregator.ComputeRouteVerdict and asserts state + verdict_reason variant equal the recorded (masked-in-equality) route_verdicts — the masked subtree is now internally consistency-checked, no circular controller run needed. (3) QA019 status test: canonical sample must record final_suite_status=success (asserting both routes COMPATIBLE in that case); the ONLY tolerated non-success is the documented bootstrap pass-1 intermediate (status=failure) and ONLY while the ADR adr_lint block is fully pending — 'unknown' is never acceptable, and a failure-status pair under any non-pending ADR claim goes red, so a pass-1 pair can never be silently cited as final while bootstrap convergence still works.

findings.description: Docs contradict the committed evidence after 67ab9ad: docs/adr/ADR-0001 'Decision' section (~lines 39-47) still says 'the committed sample is deliberately produced in variance mode (final_suite_status=unknown, route verdicts INCOMPLETE)' and the residual list (~lines 109-111) says 'A COMPATIBLE, suite-attested run report exists only under the untracked out/ area; the committed sample is variance-mode by design' — while run-report.canonical.sample.json (committed in 67ab9ad) IS a suite-attested canonical sample with final_suite_status=success and both routes COMPATIBLE, and the ADR's own Evidence header describes the pair. Additionally regen-sample.sh's comment block (~lines 170-174) claims the ADR adr_lint block 'is refreshed from the canonical sample ... without human transcription' while the code only echoes a reminder — a claimed mechanism that does not exist (AP-004), same class as QA-002's docs-lie leg.
findings.instance_fix: Rewrite the ADR Decision/residual text to the pair reality (decision may STAY pending, but the described sample state must be true); either implement the regen ADR-block refresh or reword the comment to match the echo-only behavior.
findings.class_fix: Doc statements describing committed artifacts belong in the fix-round diff review checklist: any commit changing evidence/samples/ must re-verify ADR text that describes those samples.
findings.fix_note: FIXED: ADR-0001 Decision section rewritten — boundary_decision STAYS pending (route selection is the user's later call; adr_lint block untouched, still all-pending so the linter's zero-findings test holds), but the prose now truthfully states the canonical committed sample IS suite-attested (final_suite_status=success, both routes COMPATIBLE, citable) with the variance sample as the full-equality anchor; the stale residual bullet ('COMPATIBLE report exists only under out/') replaced with the pair reality; verdict-bearing capability citations switched to the canonical sample per INV-009. regen-sample.sh's comment block made truthful: it explicitly states there is NO automatic ADR refresh — the echo is a reminder and the AdrLinter committed-sample test is the binding mechanism (truthful was the bar; no refresh implemented).

findings.description: New flake vector + dev-loop substrate corruption introduced by the fix round's nested-run tests: every controller run — including nested variance runs — unconditionally publishes out/current/spike.runsettings (run-spike.sh ~lines 814-821, pre-existing), and the new QA005/QA007/QA008/QA009 tests launch nested controllers with --run-root into REUSED name-keyed scratch dirs (SpikePaths.TestScratch). Observed post-suite state: out/current points at out/test-scratch/qa008-exit (a failed bad-solver run). The next DIRECT dotnet test therefore validates against a mutable scratch root that the same suite's QA008 test concurrently RE-RUNS a controller into (rebuilding build/* and rewriting run-context.json) while parallel collections launch digest-verified artifacts from it — Launch.Resolve digest-mismatch flakes and 'latest run' semantics silently pointing at a test fixture. Canonical controller runs are immune (SPIKE_RUN_CONTEXT set explicitly).
findings.instance_fix: Guard the out/current publish with SPIKE_CANONICAL=1 (or at minimum skip it when RUN_ROOT was overridden), and/or make TestScratch roots unique per invocation for tests that run nested controllers.
findings.class_fix: No nested/variance run may mutate shared cross-run state outside its own run root; add a test asserting out/current still points at the canonical run root after a full suite pass.
findings.fix_note: FIXED (both legs): (1) the out/current/spike.runsettings publish is guarded by SPIKE_CANONICAL=1 — nested/variance runs (any --run-root/--out/--config/--solver) never touch the shared pointer; README updated. (2) SpikePaths.TestScratch now nests every scratch root under a per-testhost-run token dir (out/test-scratch/run-<12hex>/<name>) so name-keyed roots are never reused across suite invocations — the qa008-exit-style reuse race is structurally gone; prior runs' token dirs are swept best-effort at first use. Class-fix test QA021 asserts out/current's SPIKE_RUN_CONTEXT equals the current run context and never points into a test-scratch root.

findings.description: Class-fix test gaps — three round-1 treatments are implemented but their guarding tests cannot catch regression: (1) QA-011: P05's pass predicate is delta>=1 and the QA011 test's planted foreign-sub-nonce entry cannot change any outcome whether or not ReadLedger honors probeTag (pre-planted entries cancel in before/after deltas), so a regression deleting the sub-nonce filter passes every test; the class fix's 'reproduce by forcing P05 timeout branch in a loop' was not built. (2) QA-009: the class fix demanded a route-scoped fault asserted 'unaffected end-to-end'; QA009 test stops at --phase restore exits — no test (and no code path) lets Route A reach a verdict when Route B's lock is faulted, because run-spike.sh still fail_runs the entire run on RESTORE_EXIT!=0 (attribution-only treatment, disclosed in fix_note; R4-07's non-veto property remains unmet at verdict level). (3) QA-005: the new run-level unadjudicated-failure mapping in SpikeAggregator (finalVerdicts branch keyed on terminal_state=UnadjudicatedInProcessFailure) has no induced-failure test — AP-002 wiring risk for exactly the variant round 1 called unreachable.
findings.instance_fix: (1) expose ReadLedger's tag filter to a unit test or add the forced-P05-timeout loop repro; (2) add an aggregation-level test feeding a restore receipt with mixed p01_partitions exits and assert P01(B) fail + P01(A) pass in the emitted report, and record the run-level veto residual as an explicit spec disposition; (3) drive the aggregator with a route report carrying an UnadjudicatedInProcessFailure adjudication record and assert the run-level verdict_reason variant.
findings.class_fix: A class fix is not closed until a test exists that FAILS when the fix is reverted; fix rounds must demonstrate that reversion-failure, not just green tests.
findings.fix_note: FIXED (all three gaps, each with a reversion-failing test): (1) the QA-011 tag filter is exposed as HarnessCore.ReadSentinelLedger (the same method the production P05/P08/P09 windows call); QA022 unit test builds a ledger with a same-nonce FOREIGN-probe-tag entry and asserts it is NOT counted for the probe's tag (count 1, not 2) — deleting the filter flips the count and fails the test. (2) QA022 aggregation-level test feeds a restore receipt with mixed p01_partitions exits (A=0, B=7) plus full nine-probe route reports and asserts the EMITTED report carries P01(A)=pass / P01(B)=fail, route A reasoned only by the suite channel (suite-failure) and route B probe-failure at P01(B) by manifest order; the run-level fail-closed stop on restore failure is recorded as an explicit residual disposition in ADR-0001's residual list (attribution preserved; verdicts under a broken restore deliberately not computed — spec file untouched per fix-round rules). (3) QA022 test drives the aggregator with a route report carrying an UnadjudicatedInProcessFailure adjudication record + its failing probe (child exit 10) and asserts the run-level state UNADJUDICATED_IN_PROCESS_FAILURE with verdict_reason variant unadjudicated-failure and probe P06(B) — the previously untested QA-005 mapping branch.

findings.description: CI/fresh-clone constraints that will fail committed tests if DF-001 wires ci/spike-ci.yml naively: (1) QA001_CommittedSamples_CleanTreeAncestorCommit shells out to 'git merge-base --is-ancestor <sample-commit> HEAD' and fails closed on error — a SHALLOW checkout (actions/checkout default fetch-depth 1) does not contain the samples' commit (currently d28ed5d), so the test fails spuriously; full-history checkout (fetch-depth 0) is a hard requirement, as is a git binary on the runner (EA-007). (2) The canonical run's 1800s wall-clock bound covers provisioning + cold restore + build + the 3-minute suite WITH its nested full-pipeline runs; a cold-cache CI runner 2-3x slower than the dev host approaches the bound and would synthesize WallClockExpiry. (3) A bare 'dotnet test' step without a prior controller run fails every integration test by design — the stub's run-spike.sh step must remain the entry point, and CONTROLLER_EXIT (QA-016 caveat) is the gate signal.
findings.instance_fix: Record these as requirements in the ci/spike-ci.yml stub comments (fetch-depth: 0, git present, run-spike.sh as the only test entry point, bound headroom measurement on the CI runner class) so the CI feature inherits them with DF-001.
findings.class_fix: Any committed test that shells out to repo-history-dependent tooling must state its checkout-depth requirement where the CI feature will read it.
findings.fix_note: FIXED: ci/spike-ci.yml stub now records the four hard requirements as a numbered block the CI feature inherits with DF-001 — (1) fetch-depth: 0 full-history checkout (QA001's git merge-base --is-ancestor fails closed on shallow clones; the checkout stub step names it too), (2) git binary on the runner (EA-007), (3) scripts/run-spike.sh as the sole entry point with CONTROLLER_EXIT (QA-008/QA-016 fail-closed) as the gate signal — a bare dotnet test step fails by design, (4) 1800s wall-clock headroom must be measured on the runner class before wiring, with bound changes going through the DD-006 evidence procedure. DF-001/linux-x64 markers preserved for the Inv014 stub test.

findings.description: regen-sample.sh robustness edges (all currently fail toward refusal or a visible dirty diff, hence UNCERTAIN): (1) the canonical report path is recovered by shell-globbing the controller's stderr completion line and final_suite_status by a line-grep JSON 'parse' (last quoted-key match wins) — correct today because run-level reports hold exactly one quoted key and detail strings embed the key unquoted, but a future verdict_reason detail quoting the key mis-parses silently; (2) the pair publish is two separate atomic renames — a failure between them leaves a new variance sample beside a stale canonical sample (mixed pair), detectable only in diff review; (3) the bootstrap-pass detection treats ANY non-success status (including a prerequisite-failure synthetic report's 'failure') as a committable bootstrap pass — a wrong-but-committable pair is then caught only by the NEXT suite run's masked-equality failure, not by the script.
findings.instance_fix: Parse the canonical report with a real JSON reader (dotnet is already allowlisted — a tiny 'dotnet SpikeAggregator --print-suite-status' or jq-equivalent via the contracts assembly), publish the pair via a single staged directory rename, and refuse to publish when the canonical report's route_verdicts carry prerequisite-failure variants.
findings.class_fix: Scripts that gate committable evidence must not parse JSON with line greps; route such reads through the schema-validating contracts code.
findings.fix_note: FIXED (all three edges): (1) the line-grep 'parse' is deleted — regen-sample.sh reads final_suite_status and per-route verdict variants via the new SpikeAggregator --print-suite-status mode (trust-anchor digest check + closed-schema ValidateReport + real JSON parse), using the canonical run's own built aggregator and the pinned SDK muxer resolved by the same stated rule as run-spike.sh (dotnet allowlisted; python3 was NOT used — the scripts' deny-set scan bans it, and the contracts-code route is the class fix anyway); missing aggregator artifact or readout failure refuses publication. (2) The pair (plus the directory README) is published via a single staged-directory swap (stage -> mv samples aside -> mv stage in -> drop old): a crash can leave the old pair intact or a loudly-missing dir + recoverable .old, never a silently mixed pair; leftovers trip the QA-001 dirty-tree refusal. (3) Bootstrap detection accepts ONLY a genuine stale-sample suite failure: non-success status with any route verdict variant other than suite-failure (prerequisite-failure/crash/missing-report/probe-failure synthetics) refuses publication. QA024 tests cover the readout on both committed samples (success/COMPATIBLE and unknown/suite-failure lines) and the fail-closed malformed-report path.

findings.description: Uncovered exit/report-matrix cell, fail-OPEN: a route child exit 20 (INCOMPLETE) accompanied by a present ALL-PASS report is mapped by AdjudicationStateMachine.MapExit's default branch to PrerequisiteFailure ('child exited INCOMPLETE with a typed cause in its report') WITHOUT checking that any non-pass probe or typed cause exists; VerdictAggregator.Aggregate discards that outcome (only Crash/ExitReportMismatch/MalformedReport short-circuit) and computes the verdict from the report alone. Demonstrated against the HEAD-built SpikeAggregator: receipts+all-pass route reports with --report A=...:20 --suite-status success emit route A COMPATIBLE, exit_report_matrix_outcome=consistent, aggregator exit 0. The committed matrix's only exit-20-present row requires 'present with typed cause' (schema route_outcome_algebra.exit_report_matrix), so this instance is an unclassified mismatch — INV-013 declares the table exhaustive and 'any mismatch ... is non-COMPATIBLE with its precise reason'. PRE-EXISTING since GREEN (MapExit unchanged this round; reproducible identically at 67ab9ad; the QA-022(2) childOwned scoping neither introduced nor widened it — the default branch ignores the probe list). Mitigations bounding severity: (a) no current code path can produce the contradiction — the child's exit derives from the same Results list its report is emitted from (HarnessCore.cs run exit, Fail->10/Incomplete->20/else 0 after EmitReport); (b) the controller channel stays fail-closed (run-spike.sh CONTROLLER_EXIT=1 on any child nonzero); (c) both committed samples were produced by children exiting 0. Residual risk: regen-sample.sh deliberately treats the canonical controller exit as informational (bootstrap pass-1 rationale), so a future harness bug or forged child report of this exact shape would survive the QA-019 self-consistency chain (the pair is internally consistent) — the exit/report matrix is the intended guard for that class and has this one hole. Recommended terminal disposition: DEFER (does not poison the committed evidence pair or current verdict integrity).
findings.instance_fix: In MapExit, split the Incomplete-exit case: exit 20 with a present report containing NO non-pass probe (and no typed-cause payload) returns ExitReportMismatch('INCOMPLETE exit with an all-pass report'), keeping the true PrerequisiteFailure mapping only when a non-pass probe or typed cause is present; add the corresponding row to the schema's exit_report_matrix (additive, digest re-anchor per DD-006 evidence procedure) and an induced-failure test feeding an all-pass report with exit 20 asserting non-COMPATIBLE with the mismatch reason.
findings.class_fix: Every row-shape of the committed exit/report matrix must have (a) an explicit code branch and (b) an induced-failure test; a default/fallthrough branch in MapExit that asserts report content it does not inspect is the AP-011 shape (green-checking a claim).
findings.fix_note: Deferred to backlog DF-003 by user disposition at QA round 3 gate (2026-07-22).

findings.description: Ctrl-C/SIGTERM/terminal-close does not stop a run — it silently orphans it. run-spike.sh has no trap; the outer watchdog launches the inner controller via setsid in a new session that never receives terminal signals. SIGINT kills only the outer loop; the inner controller keeps provisioning/building/aggregating up to the 1800s deadline, keeps writing out/, and for a canonical run republishes out/current minutes after the operator thinks it is dead. An immediate re-run races the orphan. README documents no abort procedure.
findings.instance_fix: Outer watchdog traps INT/TERM/HUP, forwards TERM->KILL to the inner session group (reuse existing PID-file sweep), writes a synthetic INCOMPLETE with typed OperatorInterrupt cause, exits 20; document 'stopping a run' in README.
findings.class_fix: Every supervisor that detaches children into new sessions owns a signal-forwarding trap with an induced test (SIGINT mid-build -> no inner survivor, typed synthetic report, out/current unchanged).
findings.fix_note: FIXED (merge signal-trap): run-spike.sh outer watchdog traps INT/TERM/HUP (on_operator_signal) — sweeps EVERY descendant session (inner controller + the active phase's own setsid session, via list_descendant_sessions) with TERM->KILL, writes a typed OperatorCancelled synthetic INCOMPLETE to OUT_PATH and the run-report path, exits 20. README 'Stopping a run' section added. Induced test SignalTrap_OperatorSigterm_SweepsInner_TypedReport_OutCurrentUntouched (raw-Process SIGTERM mid-run; asserts no /proc survivor referencing the run root, typed report, out/current unchanged). The 'self-enforcing' comment overstatement is fixed under MA-HI-1.

findings.description: No run-root retention/cleanup affordance. Each canonical run permanently retains an ~812MB out/<run_id>/ root (full package-cache tar copy + extracted net8 runtime + z3 archive + 5-project build output). Live dev tree holds 106 runid-* roots + 5.8GB test-scratch ~= 102GB. Nothing prunes runid-* (QA-021 sweep covers only test-scratch tokens, at next suite start). README calls out/ 'untracked run products' but never states per-run growth, safe-to-delete, or a prune command; a new user's disk fills to ENOSPC. Offboarding sub-defect: deleting roots but not out/current leaves a dangling SPIKE_RUN_CONTEXT whose error message omits the remediation the unset-var path has.
findings.instance_fix: Controller retains last N run roots (never the out/current target) or ship scripts/clean-runs.sh; README gains a disk-usage/cleanup note; add the run-spike remediation to the missing-receipt assert in Launch.cs.
findings.class_fix: Any feature minting per-run directories declares retention policy in the same spec section that mandates the untracked area; operator-surface test asserts README documents it.
findings.fix_note: FIXED (merge out-growth): canonical runs prune out/runid-* keeping the newest 5 at start (prune_run_roots; never out/current/out/cache/out/regen-*). scripts/clean-runs.sh added (operator keep-last-N tool over runid-*/regen-*, never out/current/out/cache; PRH-004-hardened, passes the scripts allowlist static scan). README 'Disk usage, cleanup, and recovery' note (~1GB/run, safe-to-delete, rm -rf out). Test CleanRuns_KeepsLastN_NeverOutCurrentOrCache. The missing-receipt remediation already exists in Launch.cs (RunContext.RequirePath, Agent 1's); additive prune test added instead.

findings.description: New-user dead end for system-wide .NET installs: resolve_sdk checks only DOTNET_ROOT/dotnet, HOME/.dotnet/dotnet, and the cached pointer — never PATH or /usr/{bin,share,lib}/dotnet. The mandated env -i strips DOTNET_ROOT, so a user with it exported still fails, and the refusal tells a user who already has 10.0.302 installed system-wide to install it, without stating the actual remedy (env -i HOME=... DOTNET_ROOT=<root> bash -p ...).
findings.instance_fix: Extend the refusal message and README with the pass-through form and state system-wide installs are not auto-discovered (or add well-known system paths as a spec-reviewed change).
findings.class_fix: Every 'checked X,Y,Z' prerequisite message under an env -i contract states how to inject the variable through that contract.
findings.fix_note: FIXED (refined per the MA-HI-1 interaction): the resolve_sdk refusal message and README name the EXPLICIT `--dotnet-root <path>` argument (the clean-environment contract strips an ambient DOTNET_ROOT, so the finding's env pass-through is replaced by an argument that survives the re-exec) and state that system-wide installs (/usr/share/dotnet, PATH) are NOT auto-discovered. run-spike.sh pre-scans --dotnet-root before resolve_sdk and consumes it in the arg parser. Test Ux3_SdkDiscovery_DocumentsExplicitDotnetRoot.

findings.description: Documented exit-20 semantics ('INCOMPLETE — synthetic report emitted') are false for a family of early exits that emit NO report: run_cmd DENY, unknown arg, missing --project/--consumer-fixture, missing NuGet.Config, missing SDK, inner-controller arg validation (--help also exits 20 as 'unknown argument'). CI wrappers/operators looking for a synthetic report find none and cannot distinguish a typo from a genuine INCOMPLETE run. provision-z3.sh exits 21/22 and regen 20/30/40 are also undocumented in the README exit tables.
findings.instance_fix: Move usage/dispatch errors to a distinct exit (e.g. 2) or amend README to state exit 20 occurs without a report for pre-run failures; add provision and regen exit rows.
findings.class_fix: Exit-contract doc test enumerates every literal 'exit N' in scripts/*.sh and asserts each appears in the README contract with a report-presence statement.
findings.fix_note: FIXED (README amended; exit codes intentionally unchanged to avoid churning test expectations): the README exit-code contract now states that exit 20 occurs WITHOUT a report for PRE-RUN failures (unknown/malformed arg, missing --project/--consumer-fixture, missing NuGet.Config, missing SDK, corrupt run-config PR-004, run_cmd DENY), and adds provision (0/20/21/22), regen (0/20/30/40), and clean-runs (0/20) exit tables with report-presence. Doc-enumeration test Ux4_EveryScriptExitCode_IsDocumentedInReadme asserts every literal `exit N` in scripts/*.sh appears in the README contract.

findings.description: Shared package cache out/cache/packages is seeded non-atomically (unsupervised tar|tar, not inside any setsid phase) and can never self-heal: a deadline KILL/ENOSPC mid-seed leaves a partial cache that the [ ! -d ] guard treats as complete forever, and every later run copies the damaged cache into its run root. Unlike the digest-verified z3/net8 archives, this cache has no integrity check; NuGet locked mode does not re-hash pre-populated extracted folders.
findings.instance_fix: Seed atomically (tar into packages.tmp.$$, then mv) and/or write a completion marker checked by the reuse guard; document rm -rf out/cache recovery.
findings.class_fix: Every cross-run cache gets atomic publish or a validity predicate + an AP-016 partial-state test; the z3 archive cache already models this.
findings.fix_note: FIXED (merge pkg-cache): out/cache/packages is seeded ATOMICALLY (tar into packages.staged.$$ via the supervised cache_copy phase, swap into place, then write the packages.complete marker LAST); the consume path trusts the cache ONLY when the marker is present (a torn/marker-less dir is announced on stderr and IGNORED — the run restores fresh). The restore-failure message and README document rm -rf out/cache recovery. Test PkgCache_MissingCompletionMarker_IsIgnoredNotConsumed.

findings.description: README direct-suite command 'dotnet test spikes/dafny-compat' only works from repo root, where spikes/dafny-compat/global.json is never resolved (muxer walks up from CWD; repo root has no global.json), so the test host runs under whatever newest SDK is installed, bypassing the 10.0.302 pin the same paragraph emphasizes. Mitigated: artifacts under test are controller-built + digest-attested and QA-013 catches source drift, so only the test-host runtime is affected.
findings.instance_fix: Document CWD explicitly (cd spikes/dafny-compat && dotnet test DafnyCompatSpike.sln) so global.json governs the direct path.
findings.class_fix: Any documented dotnet invocation relying on a non-root global.json states or structurally forces the working directory; a docs test asserts it.
findings.fix_note: FIXED: the README direct-suite command is now `cd spikes/dafny-compat && dotnet test DafnyCompatSpike.sln -noAutoResponse`, with a note that the muxer resolves global.json from the CWD so the pin only governs when run from the spike directory. Test Ux6_DirectSuite_DocumentsWorkingDirectory.

findings.description: First-run dead air: both network fetches use curl --disable -fsSL with no pre-fetch announcement; on a slow link the operator sees nothing between invocation and the end of the Z3 (~30MB) + net8 (~70MB) downloads, with no liveness signal until the 1800s watchdog.
findings.instance_fix: Print one stderr line before each fetch (source + expected size) and on completion.
findings.class_fix: Every allowlisted network fetch announces start (source+size) and completion so silence is always attributable to a hang.
findings.fix_note: FIXED: run-spike.sh announces the net8 control-runtime fetch (source + approx size) and its completion on stderr; provision-z3.sh announces the Z3 download and the digest-verify step — first-run dead air on a slow link is now attributable to the download rather than a hang.

findings.description: Spec-named Z3-execution observable solver_resource_usage_observed=true is emitted and schema-declared but asserted by NOTHING: P06 pass predicate never checks it, no test references it (zero grep hits), and both committed samples contain zero occurrences (lives only in uncommitted route reports). INV-004's RS-004 fail-closed observability rule is unenforced for this observable — if Dafny/Boogie stopped exposing resource usage (the absent-capability case that must enter INV-013 as UNADJUDICATED_IN_PROCESS_FAILURE), P06 would still pass silently. This is the in-run solver side-effect signal distinguishing real Z3 execution from a fabricated Valid outcome; surrounding evidence (removal test, decoys, P05 sentinel) is inter-invocation, not in-run.
findings.instance_fix: Assert deterministic.solver_resource_usage_observed['fixtures/ok.dfy']==true for both routes in the P06 e2e test; make the harness P06 predicate fail (absent-capability per INV-013) when the flag is false.
findings.class_fix: Every observable named in an [integration] rule's Statement/Exit contract gets a suite-wide reference check in RED review; a meta-test walks the schema deterministic-projection field list and asserts each is consumed by >=1 assertion or pass predicate.
findings.fix_note: P06 pass predicate now fails via HarnessCore.AbsentResourceUsageCapability when any task lacks solver resource usage, entering INV-013 as UNADJUDICATED absent-capability with a schema-valid adjudication record; e2e P06 test asserts deterministic.solver_resource_usage_observed['fixtures/ok.dfy']==true for both routes; unit test locks the fail direction.

findings.description: INV-004 Exit observables ('emitted evidence shows P06 pass', 'zero frontend errors') are asserted only via the child exit-code proxy, never read from the emitted report (sibling INV-005 test DOES read per_probe_results). Currently sound (harness exit derives from the same Results list its report is emitted from), so not no-op-survivable today — hence LOW — but blind to a harness regression that records P06 non-pass yet exits 0; same cell cluster as M1/DF-003 at the harness-child channel.
findings.instance_fix: Read deterministic.per_probe_results, assert the P06 entry status=='pass' and zero non-verification diagnostics for ok.dfy (mirror the Inv005 pattern).
findings.class_fix: Exit contracts phrased 'emitted evidence shows X' are asserted from the emitted evidence with the exit code as a second channel, not the sole one.
findings.fix_note: Inv004 P06 e2e now reads deterministic.per_probe_results (P06 status=='pass') and asserts zero non-verification node_table rows for ok.dfy, mirroring the Inv005 pattern — the exit code is a second channel, not the sole one.

findings.description: out/ grows without bound; no retention/GC/cleanup anywhere. Each canonical run permanently retains out/<run_id>/, and the suite it runs mints MORE full-pipeline roots directly under out/ (Inv010 two runs, Inv009 one) because they pass only --out, defaulting their run roots outside out/test-scratch (the only swept area, swept only at next suite start). Each root ~ several hundred MB (package tar + extracted net8 runtime + z3 archive + build outputs): >=4 permanently-retained roots per canonical run + ~8 scratch roots. A 14GB CI runner can approach exhaustion within one or two runs (QA-023 covered only wall-clock headroom, not disk). Disk exhaustion then aborts unsupervised sections via set -e with no report, and can trigger the cache poisoning.
findings.instance_fix: Default the bare --out nested/variance roots under the invoking suite's scratch token dir; add keep-last-N/age prune of out/runid-* and out/regen-* at canonical-run start; document rm -rf out as safe recovery.
findings.class_fix: Any mechanism minting per-run disk state ships its retention policy, enforced by a test asserting N consecutive runs leave a bounded root count.
findings.fix_note: FIXED (merge out-growth with MA-UX-2): keep-last-5 prune of out/runid-* at canonical-run start + scripts/clean-runs.sh; README disk note. Bare --out nested roots default under out/runid-* which the next canonical prune bounds; the QA-021 token-dir sweep covers test-scratch roots. Prune test asserts a bounded surviving-root count.

findings.description: Shared package cache out/cache/packages is the only cache with no integrity validation and no self-heal, populated non-atomically. Seed (tar-extract into final path, guard [ ! -d ]) runs in the inner controller's main session — NOT under run_with_env supervision — so a deadline KILL, operator SIGKILL, or disk-full mid-seed leaves a torn dir that exists forever: never re-seeded (dir exists), never digest-checked, tar-copied into every subsequent run root. tar member order is arbitrary so a package's .nupkg.metadata can land while assemblies are truncated; NuGet locked-mode trusts .nupkg.metadata without re-hashing, so restore succeeds and the fault surfaces downstream as persistent misattributed failures (build PrerequisiteFailure, or a P03 loaded-assembly digest mismatch reading as supply-chain tampering). Fail-closed but permanently wedged, no diagnostic naming the cache, no documented recovery. z3/net8 archive caches both digest-validate + self-heal; this one misses the pattern.
findings.instance_fix: Seed into out/cache/packages.staged.$$ then atomic mv; consume path treats a missing completion marker (written after verified seed) as no-cache; document rm -rf out/cache recovery in the restore-failure message.
findings.class_fix: Every shared cache gets AP-016 clean/partial/full + kill-mid-populate tests; presence-only (-d/-f without digest/marker) cache checks are a review finding.
findings.fix_note: FIXED (merge pkg-cache with MA-UX-5): completion-marker validity predicate + atomic staged seed. A deadline-KILL/ENOSPC mid-seed leaves at most a marker-less staged/partial dir the consume guard ignores and rebuilds — no torn cache is ever copied into subsequent run roots. Documented rm -rf out/cache recovery.

findings.description: SEVERITY-FLOOR CANDIDATE (nondeterministic fail-open in an enforcement observable). The sentinel ledger is a multi-writer file updated by an unlocked read-modify-write with a SHARED temp name ($LEDGER.tmp); all per-probe stubs point at the same paths. Concurrent writers are an anticipated state: QA-011's design lets an abandoned P05 leg outlive the 10s bounded join and invoke its stub concurrently with a later P08/P09 window. The QA-011 sub-nonce fix scoped COUNTING, not WRITE ATOMICITY: two concurrent invocations both read, both write the same tmp, last mv wins, one entry silently dropped. (a) the current probe's own entry can be dropped -> P05 delta>=1 fails spuriously (the QA-011 flake re-entering); (b) with a genuine solver-wiring bug, a real invocation recorded during a P08/P09 window can be ERASED by the race, letting the zero-invocation assertion (INV-011) pass — a nondeterministic FAIL-OPEN in the enforcement ledger itself.
findings.instance_fix: Make the ledger append-only and per-writer-unique: each stub invocation writes its own mktemp-named entry under sentinel/entries/, ReadLedger counts files matching the nonce/sub-nonce; or O_APPEND JSONL with a skip-and-count reader.
findings.class_fix: Any evidence file with >1 potential writer must be append-only with per-writer-unique temp names; whole-file read-modify-write-rename on shared evidence is a review finding. Co-fix with MA-HI-2 (same ledger writer).
findings.fix_note: Ledger redesigned append-only: each stub invocation writes its own mktemp-named entry file under sentinel/entries/ (HarnessCore.SentinelStubScript); no shared-tmp read-modify-write remains. ReadLedger counts entry files (+legacy embedded arrays for test fixtures) per nonce/sub-nonce; concurrency test races two real stub invocations x5 rounds and asserts zero dropped entries (MiniAudit1SentinelLedgerTests.ConcurrentStubInvocations_BothEntriesSurvive).
findings.orchestrator_note: Orchestrator raised MEDIUM->HIGH: describes a fail-open of the INV-011 zero-invocation security observable (severity-floor keywords: silent/erased/fail-open).

findings.description: Outer watchdog traps no signals: SIGINT/SIGTERM (CI job cancellation) kills the outer but the setsid'd inner session keeps running the full pipeline for the remaining wall bound. No synthetic report for the cancelled run; the orphan re-seeds out/cache and (being canonical) republishes out/current, silently repointing the dev loop after the operator started a replacement. A replacement suite's scratch sweep can also delete the orphan's still-live scratch dirs. QA-018 bounded orphan lifetime (deadline inheritance), not cancellation propagation.
findings.instance_fix: trap INT/TERM in the outer watchdog: forward TERM to the inner session group (sweep code already exists), bounded wait, escalate KILL, write synthetic INCOMPLETE with typed OperatorCancelled cause, exit nonzero.
findings.class_fix: Every supervisor detaching children into new sessions traps+forwards termination signals with an induced test.
findings.fix_note: FIXED (merge signal-trap with MA-UX-1): the outer watchdog now traps INT/TERM/HUP and forwards TERM->KILL to the inner session group before writing the OperatorCancelled synthetic and exiting nonzero; a cancelled run never reaches the post-suite out/current publish, so the dev pointer is untouched. Same induced test as MA-UX-1.

findings.description: preprocess-audit phase leaks its mktemp: run-spike.sh creates out/preprocess.XXXXXX.xml (~1-3MB MSBuild dump) and exits the phase without removing it. Invoked once per suite run by Inv001; one leaked file per run directly under out/ (missed by both the RUN_ROOT junk glob and the test-scratch sweep). Unbounded slow growth, no wrong behavior.
findings.instance_fix: mktemp under $RUN_ROOT/--scratch (or TMPDIR) and rm after the IMPORT scan (trap EXIT so refusal paths clean too).
findings.class_fix: Static script-audit test asserts every mktemp call site in scripts/*.sh pairs with a removal.
findings.fix_note: FIXED: the preprocess-audit phase traps EXIT to rm the out/preprocess.XXXXXX.xml MSBuild dump (refusal paths clean up too), so the ~1-3MB file no longer leaks under out/. Test Rb5_PreprocessAudit_TrapsMktempRemoval.

findings.description: Residual instances of QA-017's class: the inner controller still performs long-running unsupervised I/O in its own main session — the cache<->run-root packages tar copies, per-file sha_of hashing loops, and the git-ls-files manifest digest loop. A wedge there is caught only by the outer watchdog at DEADLINE+20s with generic WallClockExpiry attribution — the per-phase-attribution gap QA-017 claimed closed for 'every phase' (QA-017 named only provision + the BND-004 fetch).
findings.instance_fix: Route the two tar copies through run_with_env (a cache launch class) for per-phase setsid + typed attribution; hashing loops may stay if the copies are covered.
findings.class_fix: Enumerate supervised phases from the script's actual sequential external-command sites rather than the named phase list; keep the one-induced-hang-per-phase rule against that enumeration.
findings.fix_note: FIXED (merge unsupervised-tar with MA-XC-4): the shared-package-cache tar copies (both consume and seed) run through run_with_env under a new 'cache' launch class (setsid session + the single /proc/uptime deadline + phase_guard 'cache' attribution) via the cache_copy helper, so a wedge in the copy is independently killable rather than caught only by the outer +20s WallClockExpiry backstop. The per-file hashing loops remain in the main session (bounded, non-external).

findings.description: regen-sample.sh publish staging uses PID-suffixed names (samples.staged.$$ / samples.old.$$) and startup cleanup removes only the CURRENT PID's names — so leftovers a crashed regen leaves (adjudicated in QA-024 as tripping the QA-001 dirty-tree refusal) can never be removed by any later regen, sit in the TRACKED evidence/ parent, and permanently block all future regens behind a dirty-tree refusal whose message names neither the cause nor remediation.
findings.instance_fix: Glob-clean "$SAMPLE_DIR".staged.* and .old.* at startup before the dirty-tree check (or use fixed suffixes); mention stale staged dirs in the dirty-refusal message.
findings.class_fix: AP-017: crash-recovery cleanup must match the naming scheme of everything a crash can leave; cleanup keyed on the current process identity never cleans a prior crash.
findings.fix_note: FIXED: regen-sample.sh glob-cleans evidence/samples.staged.*/.old.* BEFORE the dirty-tree check, so a crashed regen's PID-suffixed staging leftovers (which no later regen's own-PID cleanup could remove) can no longer permanently block every future regen behind a dirty-tree refusal; the dirty-refusal message notes the auto-clean so the cause is not misattributed. Test Rb7_Regen_GlobCleansStagingBeforeDirtyCheck.

findings.description: QA-013 stale-binary guard has upgrade-surfacing blind spots: run-spike.sh builds the source manifest from a hardcoded git ls-files pattern list that omits DafnyCompatSpike.sln (a real build input), and the verifying test QA013 only iterates rows recorded in the stale run's manifest and skips rows whose file no longer exists — so files ADDED by an upgrade are invisible and DELETED files silently skipped. Upgrade shape: user pulls a version that adds a project while out/current (untracked, survives the pull) points at the pre-upgrade run -> direct dotnet test green-validates pre-upgrade binaries with QA-013 passing. Canonical runs immune (full rebuild); bounds this to the dev-loop workflow test command.
findings.instance_fix: Add *.sln (and Directory.Build.rsp) to the pattern list; in QA013, recompute the current tree's file list with the same patterns and assert set equality both directions.
findings.class_fix: Substrate-identity manifests are compared as full bidirectional set equality, never row-by-row over the recorded side only.
findings.fix_note: QA013 test now parses the pattern list from run-spike.sh (single source), recomputes the current tree's file set with those patterns, and asserts recorded==recomputed set equality BOTH directions (upgrade-added and deleted files both surface), then per-file digests. Final assertion expects '*.sln' in the pattern list per the coordination contract — RED until the shell agent adds *.sln (and Directory.Build.rsp) to run-spike.sh's git ls-files patterns; this is the single expected coordinated failure in the mid-round canonical run.

findings.description: Env-profile coverage guard not extended when QA-017 added launch classes: Ea008 asserts existence for controller/restore/build/test/harness/control/solver-identity/sentinel but NOT provision/fetch (added fix round 2). build_profile_env silently yields an EMPTY environment when a class key is absent, and require_env_key guards exist only for restore/build/test. Deleting/renaming the fetch class keeps the suite green while the BND-004 curl/tar launches run with an unconstructed environment (no decoy-first PATH, no run-root TMPDIR, no SSL passthrough). PR-006 names only the provision runtime guard; this is the fetch sibling + the committed-file coverage-test gap for both.
findings.instance_fix: Add provision and fetch to the Ea008 class-coverage list; give fetch a runtime required-key guard alongside the queued PR-006 provision guard; assert both profiles' minimum key set.
findings.class_fix: Derive the test's launch-class list from the classes the scripts actually invoke (parse run_with_env call sites, as the PRH-004 allowlist test does for commands).
findings.fix_note: Ea008 class-coverage list extended with provision+fetch and their minimum key set asserted (decoy-first PATH, run-root TMPDIR, SSL passthrough); new test derives the expected class list from the scripts' actual run_with_env call sites. The runtime require_env_key guards for provision/fetch remain the shell agent's PR-006 slice.

findings.description: PR-001's queued fix will not cure the worktree failure unless sibling gitfile-unaware .git discovery sites are fixed in the same round. Mechanical grep: HarnessCore.cs FindRepoRoot (Directory.Exists .git AND fail-open: returns spikeRoot when none found), HarnessCore.cs ReadGitHead (treats .git as directory), SpikePaths.cs RepoRoot + GitHeadCommit. In a linked worktree .git is a FILE so all misresolve independently of the SeamCommon site PR-001 names; a SeamCommon-only fix leaves the suite unable to produce a valid verdict from a worktree.
findings.instance_fix: Extract one gitfile-aware repo-root/HEAD resolver (handle .git file with 'gitdir:' + commondir) and route all sites through it when PR-001 is fixed; make HarnessCore.FindRepoRoot fail closed.
findings.class_fix: When fixing any at-scale failure, grep for sibling instances first (AP-007); fix-round checklist: grep -rn '".git"' over the spike resolves to exactly the shared resolver.
findings.fix_note: One shared gitfile-aware resolver (contracts/SpikeContracts/GitResolver.cs: FindRepoRoot/ResolveGitDir/ResolveCommonDir/ReadHeadCommit) routed through SeamCommon.FindRepoRoot, HarnessCore (startup + ReadGitState), SpikePaths.RepoRoot/GitHeadCommit. HarnessCore fails CLOSED (startup refusal) when no repo boundary exists instead of returning spikeRoot. Source-scan test asserts the ".git" literal appears only in GitResolver.cs.
findings.orchestrator_note: Expands the already-queued PR-001 fix scope to ~5 sites. Corroborated by MA-VI-5 and MA-XC-1.

findings.description: Structural-enforcement perimeter is a hardcoded 9-project list with no consistency check against the solution: SpikePaths.Projects drives INV-001 lock-pin, INV-008 graph, PRH-002 csproj scans; run-spike.sh separately hardcodes the lock-digest, P01 partition, and built-artifact lists. All complete today, but no test parses DafnyCompatSpike.sln to assert the lists match it, so a future added project silently escapes lock-pin enforcement, the Dafny-reference prohibition scan, and lock_sha256 — the fail-open shape INV-006 bans.
findings.instance_fix: Add a unit test parsing the sln, asserting its project set == SpikePaths.Projects and that every project with a packages.lock.json appears in run-spike.sh's receipt list.
findings.class_fix: Every enforcement perimeter enumerated in 2+ places gets one authoritative source (the sln) with equality tests against each copy.
findings.fix_note: New unit test parses DafnyCompatSpike.sln: project name+path set must equal SpikePaths.Projects exactly, and every project owning a packages.lock.json must appear in run-spike.sh's lock-receipt directory list (Uc4_SolutionProjectSet_MatchesSpikePathsProjects_AndLockReceiptList).

findings.description: qa-findings-*.json has no schema_version field while every sibling machine-consumed artifact carries schema_version:1 (probe-results, lens-recommendations, deferred-findings, harness-fingerprint, cross-feature-intel); checkpoint/workflow-state also lack markers. No wrong behavior today (consumers reference by ID) but the artifact with the most evolving row shape has no format discriminator. This is a Correctless-plugin artifact-format issue, not a repo bug.
findings.instance_fix: Add schema_version:1 to qa-findings (and checkpoint/workflow-state) the next time the workflow writes them.
findings.class_fix: Correctless-tooling: filed upstream as joshft/correctless#271 (redacted). No repo-side fix.
findings.fix_note: Not a repo defect (Correctless-plugin artifact-format inconsistency). Filed upstream: https://github.com/joshft/correctless/issues/271. No repo-side change.
findings.orchestrator_note: Filed upstream: https://github.com/joshft/correctless/issues/271. Not a repo defect; no fix in this spike.

findings.description: The spec-named mechanism 'duplicate keys in the raw report array are rejected before dictionary conversion' is DEAD CODE: VerdictAggregator.ToKeyedResults has zero production call sites — its only caller is a test that green-checks the unwired function (AP-002 shape). The production dictionary conversion in the aggregator uses byKey.TryAdd first-wins, silently dropping duplicates from the combined 22-entry per-probe view: a child report with P06(A)=pass then P06(A)=fail emits per_probe_results showing 'pass' for that key while the route verdict is INCOMPLETE(malformed-report) — an internally inconsistent report. The verdict itself stays fail-closed (ComputeRouteVerdict's own seen.Add), bounding severity.
findings.instance_fix: Route the aggregator's report-probe ingestion through ToKeyedResults (or inline its rejection) so a duplicate composite key surfaces as malformed-report in BOTH the verdict and the per-probe view; make controller-attested-over-child precedence an explicit ownership check, not TryAdd ordering.
findings.class_fix: Every spec-named enforcement component has >=1 test through the production entry point (aggregator binary / harness executable), not the function in isolation; add a production-call-site scan for named enforcement functions.
findings.fix_note: Aggregator ingestion routes every parsed route report through VerdictAggregator.ToKeyedResults (duplicate composite key -> whole report malformed in verdict AND per_probe_results); combined 22-entry view now selects by the manifest's committed owner column (controller/aggregator vs route-child), never TryAdd ordering. Locking test launches the aggregator binary with a duplicated P06(B) key (MiniAudit1AggregatorTests.Vi1_DuplicateCompositeKey_...).

findings.description: Run-level verdict_reason mislabels prerequisite/incomplete faults as probe-failure: ComputeRouteVerdict returns ProbeFailure for ANY non-pass probe including Incomplete entries with typed prerequisite causes (solver-unavailable, sentinel missing, unreadable fixture, P04 identity faults). Aggregate computes the correct PrerequisiteFailure via MapExit's default branch then DISCARDS it (only Crash/ExitReportMismatch/MalformedReport short-circuit) and relabels via ComputeRouteVerdict. Net: a solver-missing run reads variant:probe-failure, first_failing:P06(A) where INV-013's union + schema exit-20 row require prerequisite-failure with a typed cause. State stays INCOMPLETE (fail-closed) but the taxonomy lies — same class as QA-005 ('typed causes lie'), fixed only on the controller-synthetic channel; this aggregator relabel is unrecorded and adjacent to DF-003/M1.
findings.instance_fix: In ComputeRouteVerdict branch on the first non-passing probe's status: Fail->ProbeFailure, Incomplete->PrerequisiteFailure carrying the probe key + detail; or in Aggregate honor MapExit's PrerequisiteFailure for exit-20 children instead of discarding.
findings.class_fix: One induced-failure test per (probe-status x verdict-reason-variant) pair asserting the run-level variant matches the induced fault class — extend QA-005's class fix from controller to aggregator channel. Land with DF-003 (same MapExit/Aggregate cell cluster).
findings.fix_note: ComputeRouteVerdict branches on the first non-passing probe's STATUS: Fail -> probe-failure; Incomplete -> prerequisite-failure carrying the probe key + its typed detail. Unit tests per status-variant pair; aggregator-channel induced test via the P04-override run (prerequisite-failure naming P04(shared)). DF-003/QA-025 exit-20 cell remains DEFERRED (untouched).

findings.description: INV-002's clause 'any loaded Dafny/Boogie-family assembly outside that mapping fails P03' is structurally unenforced at capture: CaptureRuntimeIdentity filters GetAssemblies() with 'if IsDynamic || !packageAssets.ContainsKey(name) continue' and skips empty-location loads — so a family-named assembly loaded from OUTSIDE the deps.json mapping (LoadFile from a stray path, byte[] load, resolver fallback) never enters the set and cannot fail the equality. The test exercises only P03Evaluator with synthetic evidence; the capture layer guarantees it never sees such an extra (AP-011 route-around). QA-004's ratified universe predicate arguably sanctions the filter, and reaching the gap needs a load channel the harness lacks today — but the clause can only ever pass vacuously.
findings.instance_fix: Add a second non-filtered sweep in CaptureRuntimeIdentity: any non-dynamic loaded assembly with a Dafny*/Boogie* family name (or empty location) not a deps.json asset fails P03 with a typed detail, independent of the declared universe.
findings.class_fix: Every 'X outside the mapping fails' clause runs its check on the UNFILTERED observation source, with a test injecting an out-of-universe instance at the capture layer.
findings.fix_note: CaptureRuntimeIdentity performs a second NON-filtered sweep: any non-dynamic Dafny*/Boogie*-named assembly with empty location, a name outside the deps.json asset map, or a location outside the sanctioned roots (harness output tree / spike-local packages) fails P03 with a typed out-of-universe detail before the equality evaluation. Induced-failure test loads a real family dll from a stray path via the new --load-extra-assembly harness hook in a child-harness run.

findings.description: AdjudicationStateMachine.MapExit returns RouteOutcome(RouteState.Compatible,null,null) as a mere 'exit/report consistent' sentinel — a RouteOutcome reading COMPATIBLE that does not mean a route verdict. Both current consumers discard it, but any future caller reading outcome.State from MapExit directly converts a consistency check into a COMPATIBLE verdict — a latent fail-open API shape in the spike's most sensitive type.
findings.instance_fix: Change MapExit's consistent-success cell to a distinct value (nullable outcome or ExitConsistency.Consistent); adjust the two call sites.
findings.class_fix: Never encode 'no finding' as the optimistic member of a verdict enum; consistency routines get their own result types.
findings.fix_note: MapExit returns RouteOutcome? and the consistent-success cell is NULL — consistency can no longer be read as COMPATIBLE by any caller; both call sites (Aggregate, aggregator exit_report_matrix) updated; locking test asserts the cell is null.

findings.description: HarnessCore.FindRepoRoot is a second gitfile-unaware Directory.Exists(.git) walk (same defect as PR-001's SeamCommon site). In a linked worktree the walk finds no .git dir, ReadGitState falls back to ('unknown',false), and route reports emitted outside a controller run root carry git_commit_id:'unknown'. Fail-closed for committed samples (QA-001's 40-hex assert rejects), but the AP-007 sibling PR-001 would miss if fixed only at SeamCommon.
findings.instance_fix: Cover both sites with the PR-001 fix (shared gitfile-aware root finder; a .git FILE with gitdir: resolves like a directory).
findings.class_fix: When PR-001 lands, git grep '\.git' over the spike and route all probes through the single resolver; add the worktree case to the fix's test.
findings.fix_note: HarnessCore's gitfile-unaware walk deleted; ReadGitState resolves HEAD via GitResolver.ReadHeadCommit (gitdir: + commondir aware). Covered by the fabricated linked-worktree test (MiniAudit1GitfileTests).

findings.description: Every aggregator input gating COMPATIBLE is run-bound except the decisive one: restore/build receipts are run_id+nonce-bound and route reports run_id-bound, but final_suite_status arrives as a bare argv string (--suite-status). An out-of-band re-invocation of the built aggregator against a real run's on-disk receipts/reports with a forged --suite-status success mints a schema-valid, internally consistent run report with COMPATIBLE verdicts despite a failing/never-run suite; QA-019's recompute tests would not reject it if committed — only DD-008 regen provenance + human diff review stand in the way. QA-019 stopped at internal consistency; this is the remaining asymmetry.
findings.instance_fix: Controller emits a nonce-bound suite receipt (run_id, nonce, suite exit, runsettings/source-manifest digest) after the test phase; aggregator derives final_suite_status from the validated receipt, treats --suite-status as at most a cross-check, refuses on mismatch.
findings.class_fix: Any input in the verdict formula is receipt-bound to the run mint, never a bare argv/env claim; a test enumerates the aggregator argv surface against the receipt-bound set.
findings.fix_note: Aggregator side: final_suite_status DERIVES from the nonce-bound suite receipt at receipts/suite-receipt.json ({run_id, nonce, suite_exit, source_manifest_sha256}; contract documented at RunLayout.SuiteReceiptRelativePath); --suite-status is a cross-check REFUSED on mismatch/stale receipt; an unvalidated bare success claim without a receipt is downgraded to unknown (fail-closed, COMPATIBLE unreachable). Controller-side emission is the shell agent's matching half. Tests: forged claim refused, stale run_id refused, downgrade + receipt-derived success proven.
