CONTROLLER HALF (item A) now implemented: run-spike.sh emits receipts/suite-receipt.json (write-temp-then-mv) after the test phase of a canonical run only, with {run_id, nonce, suite_exit, source_manifest_sha256} bound to the current run; the canonical run reaches 'suite: success ... COMPATIBLE'. Verified by the canonical run.

findings.description: P04's installed-binary-vs-provisioning cross-check (QA-002 fix) is silently skipped when --solver points outside the standard layout: the guard means an override path gets banner-string + retained-archive checks only, so a fake solver echoing 'Z3 version 4.12.1' yields P04 pass with the fake's digest recorded as executed_solver_sha256. Bounded to LOW: any --solver override flips run-spike.sh to variance mode (all route verdicts INCOMPLETE), so no COMPATIBLE is reachable — the residue is a wrong-reason P04 pass in non-citable variance evidence (AP-010 shape).
findings.instance_fix: For a --solver override, record P04 as pass-with-downgrade-noted or Incomplete with detail 'override path: provisioning cross-check not applicable'; never an unqualified pass unless the binary.sha256 comparison ran.
findings.class_fix: Any probe whose pass predicate conditionally skips a named sub-check records which sub-checks ran; a test asserts the skip path cannot produce an unqualified pass.
findings.fix_note: P04 with a --solver override outside the standard layout records Incomplete with 'override path: provisioning cross-check not applicable' — an unqualified pass is unreachable unless the binary.sha256 comparison ran. Test copies the genuine provisioned binary to a non-standard path (banner+digest genuine) and asserts P04 incomplete + prerequisite-failure verdicts.

findings.description: PR-001 gitfile-unaware detection has three unrecorded sibling instances beyond SeamCommon: HarnessCore.FindRepoRoot (Directory.Exists .git), HarnessCore.ReadGitHead (reads .git/HEAD as directory layout -> wrong repo's HEAD or throws in a worktree), and SpikePaths RepoRoot + GitHeadCommit (feed the QA-001 ancestor test and Inv009's independent-HEAD check, so in a worktree they consult the OUTER repo's HEAD). A SeamCommon-only fix leaves the class in the harness evidence-binding fallback and the suite's git identity checks; worktree runs stay poisoned.
findings.instance_fix: Make all four sites gitfile-aware (treat a .git regular file as a boundary, resolve gitdir:/commondir before reading HEAD/refs); apply in SeamCommon, HarnessCore, SpikePaths in the same commit as PR-001.
findings.class_fix: One shared repo-root/HEAD resolver (contracts-side, mirrored test-side) instead of four walks; a suite test fabricates a linked-worktree-shaped tree and asserts each resolver returns the worktree root+HEAD.
findings.fix_note: All four sibling sites (SeamCommon, HarnessCore FindRepoRoot/ReadGitHead, SpikePaths RepoRoot/GitHeadCommit) now consume the single shared resolver; worktree-shaped tree test asserts each surface returns the worktree root+HEAD (incl. commondir packed-refs leg), plus the AP-007 source-scan lock.

findings.description: env-audit receipt silently loses launches audited inside command substitutions: run_with_env accumulates AUDIT_ENTRIES in shell state and rewrites env-audit.json on every launch; SDK_ACTUAL=$(run_with_env build ... dotnet --version) and the per-partition P01 restores run in subshells, so their audit entries are written then overwritten by the next parent-side audit_launch rebuilding from the parent's stale AUDIT_ENTRIES. Every run's final env-audit.json omits the dotnet --version build-class launch while EA-008/README claim the receipt records the exact dictionary at each launch. No verdict impact; evidence-completeness gap in an authoritative record.
findings.instance_fix: Capture dotnet --version via a temp file instead of command substitution (or append audit entries to a file/FIFO rather than a shell variable).
findings.class_fix: audit_launch is append-only against the on-disk file (RMW of the entries array or NDJSON append) so process topology never drops entries; a test asserts the env-audit launch count matches the known plan.
findings.fix_note: FIXED (instance_fix): `dotnet --version` is captured via a temp file ($RUN_ROOT/receipts/.sdk-version) instead of a command substitution, so run_with_env build runs in the PARENT shell and its env-audit.json entry survives (it was previously overwritten when the next parent-side audit_launch rebuilt from the parent's stale AUDIT_ENTRIES). The named build-class launch is restored; the p01-restore subshell audits remain a minor documented edge (they run only on restore failure).

findings.description: Layer-1 allowlist drifted from spec without a spec change: spec PRH-004 enumerates bash,dotnet,curl,sha256sum,tar,unzip,git,mkdir,mktemp,mv,chmod,setsid,kill,sleep; the implementation (three scripts' ALLOWLIST + BootstrapAllowlist.Commands) adds rm (needed by QA-014 cleanup). The static test now certifies a list the spec does not state — the three-point-change discipline is broken at the spec point, and the QA-014 fix notes never record the widening as adjudicated.
findings.instance_fix: Record the rm addition where the workflow records spec deviations (spec amendment or the ADR-0001 residual list), citing QA-014.
findings.class_fix: Any edit to a spec-enumerated list carries an explicit adjudication record in the round artifact; the static test comment cites the spec section it certifies.
findings.fix_note: FIXED (ADR-side residual now recorded, completing the C# enforcement-copy comment Agent 1 added): docs/adr/ADR-0001 residual list records the `rm` layer-1 allowlist widening, citing QA-014, as the adjudicated PRH-004 spec deviation (three-point-change discipline's spec point).

findings.description: Two inner-controller inter-phase steps run outside per-phase setsid supervision + typed-cause machinery QA-017 retrofitted: the package-cache tar copies (shared cache->run root every nested run; run root->cache first run). A hang blocks until the outer watchdog's DEADLINE+20s generic WallClockExpiry; a tar failure aborts via set -e with no synthetic report (the untyped-abort shape QA-017 eliminated for fetch/extract). The shared out/cache these copies use is populated non-atomically by whichever run first finds it absent, so a cold-cache race between concurrent runs can partially materialize it (no lock/staged rename).
findings.instance_fix: Route both tar copies through run_with_env (a cache launch class) for setsid + attribution; populate out/cache via write-to-temp-then-rename.
findings.class_fix: Extend RS-012 induced-failure inventory from 'one hang per named phase' to 'every foreground external-command site in the inner controller is under a supervised phase', enforced by a static scan.
findings.fix_note: FIXED (merge unsupervised-tar with MA-RB-6): both cache tar copies routed through the supervised 'cache' launch class (cache_copy under run_with_env). The shared cache is populated write-to-staged-then-swap with a completion marker (MA-UX-5/MA-RB-2), so the cold-cache partial-materialization race is closed (a marker-less dir is ignored).

findings.description: Exit-code collision: run_with_env returns 124 for a supervisor-detected timeout but otherwise returns the child's own exit code — so a phase child that itself exits 124 (timeout(1) convention; conceivable from a test-host/tool wrapper, never from the spike's own 0/10/20/21/22/30 contracts) is misclassified by phase_guard as WallClockExpiry, emitting a wall-clock INCOMPLETE with a false typed cause. Fail-closed direction preserved (INCOMPLETE either way) and no current in-repo child can produce 124 on its own — hence UNCERTAIN. QA-005's per-variant tests cannot catch it (variant right, only cause wrong).
findings.instance_fix: Distinguish the supervisor timeout signal from the child exit out-of-band (run_with_env sets a SPIKE_PHASE_TIMED_OUT flag the caller checks) instead of overloading 124; keep child exit codes verbatim for cause typing.
findings.class_fix: Never encode supervisor state in the child-exit-code channel; any in-band sentinel needs a collision note + a test feeding a child that exits the sentinel value asserting the non-timeout cause.
findings.fix_note: FIXED: phase_guard classifies a wall-clock timeout by the OUT-OF-BAND SPIKE_PHASE_TIMED_OUT flag (reset per phase at the top of run_with_env, set only by supervise_phase on a genuine deadline kill), never by overloading exit code 124 — a phase child that exits 124 on its own is no longer misclassified as WallClockExpiry. Test Xc5_PhaseGuard_UsesTimeoutFlag_NotExit124.

findings.description: QA-017 added provision/fetch launch classes but the EA-008 unit test's class enumeration was not extended (still controller/restore/build/test/harness/control/solver-identity/sentinel), and no test constrains the provision/fetch profiles' shape (decoy-first PATH, run-root TMPDIR). Deleting/gutting either class in the committed file goes green at the unit level; only the e2e QA-017 path notices indirectly. PR-006 records the missing runtime require_env_key guard for provision; this is the missing test-side armor for the same new classes.
findings.instance_fix: Add provision+fetch to the enumerated class list and assert their minimum key set in Ea008.
findings.class_fix: Derive the test's class list from the controller's actual run_with_env call-site classes so a new class fails the test until profiled.
findings.fix_note: Same treatment as MA-UC-2 (merge group ea008-classes): provision+fetch enumerated with minimum-key assertions and the call-site-derived class list test.

findings.description: evidence_schema_version is an unguarded duplicated literal in the shell: run-spike.sh write_failed_report hardcodes 'evidence_schema_version':2 while computing the schema digest fresh from the file. The C# emitters hardcode 2 but self-validate against the closed schema (const:2) before writing; shell-synthesized INCOMPLETE reports are never schema-validated at emission. On the next schema bump the shell literal is the copy nothing forces to move: synthetics would declare v2 alongside a v3 digest, caught only if a consumer happens to ValidateReport a synthetic. Same shape as PR-007 but outside its named scope.
findings.instance_fix: Have write_failed_report read the version from schema/evidence-schema.json (already read for its digest), or validate one synthetic report per run.
findings.class_fix: Extend the PR-007 fix sweep to all shell-side duplications of schema/pin constants; a test greps scripts for hardcoded values with a committed source of truth and asserts each equals its file.
findings.fix_note: FIXED: write_failed_report emits evidence_schema_version from EVIDENCE_SCHEMA_VERSION, read from schema/evidence-schema.json (the file already read for its digest), not a hardcoded literal a future schema bump would leave stale beside a fresh digest. Test Xc7_WriteFailedReport_UsesSchemaVersionVariable.

findings.description: The class-2 equality domain is only ever exercised against RUN-level reports. Both committed samples are kind:run-report and the INV-010 run-twice test projects only the run-spike --out run report. ROUTE reports — which carry most schema-declared class-2 fields (node_table, option_manifest_readback, normalization_canary_observations, closure_sets, verification_target_sets, solver_outcome_enums, solver_resource_usage_observed, loaded_assembly_identities, resolved_package_versions, sentinel_ledger_outcomes) — are never run through DeterministicProjection equality anywhere. Oracle-pinned values are indirectly stable, but explicitly observational class-2 content (canary effective values per OQ-003, P07 observed ranges, per-probe detail strings, map/array shape) can flap across runs with no detector — contradicting INV-010's 'identical deterministic projections' and RS-005's flap-is-blocking discipline, since a route-level flap surfaces in the run report only if it flips a probe status.
findings.instance_fix: Extend the run-twice test + committed-sample machinery to route reports: project route-a.json/route-b.json from both runs and assert equality (and/or commit RID-scoped route-report samples).
findings.class_fix: For every report kind the schema declares (route/run/control), require >=1 projection-equality consumer; a coverage test fails when a schema-declared kind has no cross-run equality check.
findings.fix_note: Run-twice determinism extended to ALL schema-declared kinds: route-a/route-b/control-a/control-b projections asserted equal across the two consecutive runs; kind->consumer coverage meta-test (schema-digest-anchored) fails when a declared kind has no equality consumer. DeterministicProjection canonicalizes the sentinel nonce echoed inside class-2 sentinel_ledger_outcomes to <sentinel-nonce> (same codex R2-1 rule as run_id/run-root).

findings.description: Several schema-declared partition fields have NO producer anywhere, so the effective equality domain and binding record are silently narrower than the schema declares (the drift INV-009's schema-file derivation was meant to make reviewable). Confirmed by grep + real reports: (a) class-2 deps_json_digests_normalized — never emitted, equality passes by absence==absence; (b) class-1 raw_digests_of_path_bearing_generated_files and run_product_paths_concrete — never emitted; (c) class-1 glibc_floor (EA-002 'recorded in evidence') exists only in z3-pin.json (PR-007: not consumed) — never reaches any report; (d) hostfxr_identity_concrete/corelib_identity_concrete emitted only by ControlCore — the route harness computes hostfxr/CoreLib identity for the P03 predicate then discards it, so route reports contain no hostfxr/corelib strings (codex R4-03 identity validated but unauditable). report_schema marks all optional and DeterministicProjection projects only instance-present fields, so no existing test can see any of this.
findings.instance_fix: Emit the missing fields (per-route normalized .deps.json digests into class 2 with raw in binding; run-product paths; glibc floor from pin config into run-report binding; hostfxr/corelib identity into route-report binding) and regenerate the sample pair.
findings.class_fix: Per-kind field-coverage test: a committed (schema-digest-anchored) map of report-kind -> required partition fields asserted against both committed samples and a fresh run, so 'declared but never produced' fails loudly.
findings.fix_note: Producers added for every declared-but-unproduced field: route reports emit deps_json_digests_normalized (class 2; CRLF->LF + run-root tokenized) with raw_digests_of_path_bearing_generated_files in binding, run_product_paths_concrete, and the P03-computed hostfxr/corelib_identity_concrete (persisted, no longer discarded); the aggregator emits glibc_floor into run-report binding identity sourced from the startup-validated z3-pin.json. Per-kind field-coverage meta-test (schema-digest-anchored map, asserted against fresh route/control reports + a fresh in-test aggregation) locks 'declared => produced'. NO schema change was needed (all fields were already declared in v2), so no digest bump; committed run-report samples carry no new class-2 fields and remain valid. Sample regen (shell agent) will additionally pick up glibc_floor in binding (class 1, outside equality).

findings.description: The 'run_id appears nowhere in the projection' guarantee is enforced inside the CONSUMER: DeterministicProjection string-replaces run_directory, out/<run_id>, and run_id in the serialized projection. The spec-mandated test therefore cannot fail regardless of producer behavior — a harness leaking run-root-dependent paths into class-2 fields is laundered at projection time, not caught. Residuals: an absolute-path prefix outside the scrubbed substrings survives into the sample's raw JSON, caught by the hygiene grep only for /home,/Users,/tmp,C:\ roots (not /opt,/var); the run-dir basename without its out/ prefix isn't in the replacement list. Determinism impact on the pinned single-host setup is nil.
findings.instance_fix: Add a producer-side raw-report assertion: no class-2 subtree value contains the run_id, run_directory string, or a rooted path outside <run-root>; keep the projection scrub as defense-in-depth.
findings.class_fix: Every 'canonicalized before X' rule is tested on the producer's raw artifact, never solely on the output of a consumer that re-canonicalizes.
findings.fix_note: FIXED (additive producer-side assertion): Ed3_RawRouteReport_Class2_HasNoRunIdOrStrayRoots asserts the substrate run's route-a/route-b class-2 (deterministic) subtree carries no run_id, no run_directory key, no concrete run-root path, and no rooted host path (/home,/Users,/tmp,/opt,/var) — the guarantee is checked on the PRODUCER's raw artifact, not laundered by the consumer's projection scrub. The projection scrub remains as defense-in-depth.

findings.description: The RID-scoped sample-equality test's 'loud skip' is a GREEN PASS: on non-linux-x64, CommittedSample_Exists_AndFreshRunProjectionEqual_RidScoped writes one ITestOutputHelper line and returns, so the suite records the fresh-run-equality guarantee as PASSED with zero comparisons executed. xunit output lines are invisible in default console/CI summaries; nothing distinguishes 'equality proven' from 'not attempted' (AP-013 phantom-execution; spec wants a loud 'skipped: unproven RID'). Theoretical on the pinned linux-x64 host, real the moment DF-001 wires CI or a contributor runs elsewhere.
findings.instance_fix: Make the scope check produce a genuinely skipped/inconclusive outcome (SkippableFact/Assert.Skip or a hard failure naming the unproven RID) so the summary records it distinctly from pass.
findings.class_fix: No environment-scoped test reports success when its scoped body did not run; scope-gate exits map to a result state distinct from pass, asserted by a meta-test.
findings.fix_note: RID gate is now SpikePaths.RequireProvenRid(): throws loudly ('skipped: unproven RID <rid>') on non-linux-x64 — a genuinely non-pass outcome; the silent early-return pattern is banned from test sources by a meta-scan (Ed4_NoSilentRidEarlyReturn_InTestSources).

findings.description: The controller's documented contract is 'env -i HOME=$HOME bash -p', and the script claims 'Hardening is self-enforcing' — but only PRIVILEGED mode is enforced ($- must contain p). The env -i (clean environment) half is NEVER enforced; bash -p only ignores BASH_ENV/ENV, every other inherited variable survives and several steer the run: DOTNET_ROOT (resolve_sdk reads ${DOTNET_ROOT}/dotnet FIRST, so an inherited value selects the muxer for restore/build/test and every dotnet exec — the ambient resolution TB-004 forbids; a version-spoofing wrapper shimming --version to the pinned string while delegating to a different .NET 10 SDK passes P02+P03, voiding the AP-015 exact-SDK pin), SPIKE_RID_OVERRIDE (forwarded to provision-z3.sh -> exit 21 'RID not supported' on a good host -> spurious INCOMPLETE with a false diagnostic; an internal fault-hook honored from the inherited env), SPIKE_PARENT_DEADLINE (a small inherited value caps the deadline -> watchdog kills a healthy run early). The -p-only check passes under 'bash -p scripts/run-spike.sh' (no env -i) in CI, a dev shell, or a poisoned profile.
findings.instance_fix: After the -p check, assert a clean environment: re-exec self under env -i HOME=$HOME PATH=/usr/bin:/bin bash -p with a sentinel proving the re-exec, and hard-fail if any non-allowlisted variable (DOTNET_ROOT, SPIKE_RID_OVERRIDE, SPIKE_PARENT_DEADLINE, SPIKE_CANONICAL, GIT_*, NUGET_PACKAGES, SSL_CERT_*) is present at canonical entry. SPIKE_RID_OVERRIDE must not be honored from an inherited environment.
findings.class_fix: Treat the controller entry environment as untrusted input with an explicit allowlist (construct rather than inherit-then-override); make the 'clean environment' contract a mechanism that runs (a test asserts inheriting DOTNET_ROOT/SPIKE_RID_OVERRIDE/SPIKE_PARENT_DEADLINE into a bash -p (no env -i) invocation cannot change the toolchain or force a prerequisite failure). Fix the AP-004 overstatement in the 'self-enforcing' comment.
findings.fix_note: FIXED (with a documented, stronger-form deviation): run-spike.sh re-execs the canonical entry under `env -i` (allowlist: HOME, PATH, the re-exec sentinel, and the fail-CLOSED-only sanctioned hooks SPIKE_PARENT_DEADLINE and SPIKE_RID_OVERRIDE), then asserts no non-allowlisted variable is present (a forged sentinel that skipped the re-exec fails closed, exit 30). The FALSE-COMPATIBLE vectors (DOTNET_ROOT, DOTNET_HOST_PATH, NUGET_PACKAGES, SSL_CERT_*, GIT_*) are STRIPPED. The header 'self-enforcing' claim is corrected to name only the two enforced mechanisms (AP-004). DEVIATION from instance_fix, recorded in ADR-0001 residuals: SPIKE_RID_OVERRIDE is PRESERVED because the committed round-1 QA-005 test drives it as the provisioning fault hook and it can only ever fail CLOSED (spurious INCOMPLETE, never a false COMPATIBLE) — I must not restructure Agent 1's tests; and the finding's proposed DOTNET_ROOT env pass-through for system-wide installs (which would reopen the hijack) is replaced by an explicit --dotnet-root <path> argument (MA-UX-3) that survives the re-exec and cannot be ambiently inherited (a strictly stronger form). Test Hi1_InheritedDotnetRoot_CannotHijackToolchain (fake dotnet + marker: marker never created => real SDK used; on reversion the fake runs and the marker appears). Smoke-verified: a poisoned DOTNET_ROOT via `bash -p` (no env -i) is stripped and the real SDK restores. The full canonical run stays green (nested fixtures QA-017/QA-018/Inv010/Inv009/Inv006/Inv014 all spawn nested controllers under the sanctioned env, unaffected).

findings.description: The nonce-recording sentinel stub (the observable P08/P09 zero-invocation gate and P05 depend on) builds its ledger JSON by string concatenation and appends with sed, inadequately escaped: each arg is 'escaped' by tr -d '\\"' (strips only backslash + double-quote), then interpolated into a sed REPLACEMENT (s|...|...|). Any & in the entry expands to the whole matched ledger text; any | terminates the s delimiter; a ] or embedded newline yields malformed JSON. The recorded args are the real z3 argv and the run root (hence TMPDIR + temp SMT paths), which carry such characters when the repo/run-root path does (checkout under a dir containing & | ] or whitespace). Corruption makes post-run ReadLedger (JsonDocument.Parse) throw -> spurious UNADJUDICATED P05 failure — silent corruption of the very ledger that is the INV-003/INV-011 security observable. Fail-closed (a corrupted ledger cannot produce a false zero-invocations), so no verdict flips to COMPATIBLE; UNCERTAIN only because Boogie's live z3 argv carrying a run-root metachar wasn't confirmed without executing. The escaping defect itself is certain.
findings.instance_fix: Do not build/append JSON with tr+sed; append entries with a tool emitting valid JSON regardless of arg bytes (base64-encode each arg, or newline-delimited records the reader tolerates), never untrusted text on a sed replacement side. Co-fix with MA-RB-3 (same ledger writer).
findings.class_fix: Ban string-interpolated construction of the ledger/records (AP-008) across the shell layer; the sentinel ledger is acceptance-relevant so its writer/reader round-trip arbitrary bytes and the reader handles a malformed line per a declared contract (skip-and-count or fail-loud), not whole-file Parse that one bad append zeroes.
findings.fix_note: sed/tr JSON construction removed: entry files carry space-separated base64 fields (b64(nonce) b64(probe:tag) b64(argv...)), round-tripping arbitrary bytes; declared malformed-line contract is skip-and-COUNT with P05/P08/P09 pass predicates failing loudly on malformed>0. Tests: MetacharacterArgv_RoundTripsWithoutCorruption (& | ] newline quotes backslashes empty-arg), MalformedEntryFile_IsCountedNotSwallowed.
findings.orchestrator_note: Same sentinel-ledger writer as MA-RB-3; fix together (append-only + byte-safe encoding).

findings.description: json_escape (run-spike.sh) escapes only \ and " — not JSON control chars (\n,\t, bytes <0x20). Used to write path-input-derived and tool-output-derived values into every controller JSON the aggregator parses: git-state.json, restore/build-receipt argv+sdk_version (SDK_ACTUAL is dotnet --version output), env-audit.json, and the synthetic write_failed_report. A --run-root path with a raw newline, or a hijacked dotnet --version (see MA-HI-1) with a control char, yields invalid JSON. Consumers fail closed to INCOMPLETE, so no wrong verdict, but a benign path/tool value becomes malformed evidence rather than a correctly-escaped record.
findings.instance_fix: Extend json_escape to escape control characters (\n,\t,\r and \u00XX for other C0 bytes), or route evidence emission through a JSON-emitting tool.
findings.class_fix: Prohibit hand-rolled JSON serialization for any artifact crossing back into schema-validated contracts; centralize shell JSON in one escaper with a control-char/metacharacter unit test.
findings.fix_note: FIXED: json_escape now escapes the realistic JSON control characters (newline, CR, tab, backspace, formfeed) in addition to backslash/quote, so a path- or tool-output-derived value carrying a raw newline (a --run-root path, a hijacked `dotnet --version`) no longer yields invalid JSON. Test Hi3_JsonEscape_HandlesControlCharacters. Rarer C0 bytes remain raw (best-effort in pure bash; noted in the code comment).

findings.description: The package-cache atomicity + completion-marker fix (round-1 HIGH) is locked ONLY by a source-scan (MiniAudit1OperatorTests PkgCache_ConsumeGuardsMarker Assert.Contains on literal strings), not the AP-016 behavioral partial-state / kill-mid-seed test its class_fix demanded. A runtime-semantic regression (torn cache consumed, marker written prematurely, failed mv) would stay green. Fix works today (green canonical run) but is not behaviorally locked.
findings.instance_fix: Add an AP-016 behavioral test: seed a cache under isolated --out, delete the marker (or truncate a member), run consume, assert the marker-less dir is ignored + a fresh restore runs; plus a kill-mid-seed test asserting no torn dir is consumed.
findings.fix_note: FIXED (behavioral, replacing the source-scan): the pkg-cache consume guard is factored into shared_cache_is_trusted (used by BOTH the production consume path and a new test-drivable --phase cache-consume, no drift). New test MaIdR21_PkgCache_AtomicSeed_TornDirIgnoredNotConsumed seeds a cache under an isolated --out-dir via the real atomic publish (--phase cache-publish; marker written LAST), proves consume TRUSTS it, then deletes the marker (== the kill-mid-seed torn state) and proves the guard IGNORES it (a fresh restore runs), never consuming a torn dir. Round-1 PkgCache source-scan updated to the new function/mv -T structure.

findings.description: The round-1 prune introduced an unguarded destructive rm -rf over the SHARED out/ substrate with NO lock. prune_run_roots and clean-runs.sh protect only the single caller's own root. Under concurrent canonical runs (an anticipated state - the cache is hardened for it), run B's start-prune globs out/runid-* including run A's canonical + live nested-suite roots; if >=5 newer roots exist, A's root is rm -rf'd WHILE A is live -> cascade failures / evidence loss. Exactly the live-root-deletion class agent 2 hit; its test guard (--out-dir isolation) protects the test, not real concurrent/operator use.
findings.instance_fix: Take a coarse out/-level lock (mkdir/flock on the allowlist) around prune + a run's lifetime, or skip any root whose .inner-pid names a live PID (and roots newer than a grace window). Add a concurrent-runs induced test: prune while a planted root has a live PID -> not deleted.
findings.fix_note: FIXED (out/-level lock backbone): a coarse mkdir-based advisory lock (out/.lock, acquire_out_lock/release_out_lock; holder pid recorded, stale holder reclaimed via kill -0) now guards the canonical-start prune AND the cache seed publish. prune_run_roots additionally SKIPS any out/runid-* whose .inner-pid names a LIVE controller (a concurrent canonical run). New test MaRbR21_Prune_SkipsLiveRunRoot_AndAnnouncesCount plants a live-pid root as the OLDEST and asserts it survives keep-2 (would be deleted on reversion), driving the REAL prune_run_roots via --phase prune-run-roots.

findings.description: The documented disk tool leaves the LARGEST consumer unreclaimable. clean-runs.sh + auto-prune only touch out/runid-*/regen-*, but every canonical run's suite phase populates out/test-scratch/<token> via nested-controller tests (9.7GB on the live tree vs 5.4GB runid/regen). QA-021 bounds test-scratch across suite runs but gives NO on-demand reclaim; the only documented recourse is rm -rf out, which also destroys out/cache and forces a cold restore/provision. The disk-exhaustion HIGH is only partially closed.
findings.instance_fix: Extend clean-runs.sh to prune stale out/test-scratch/* token dirs (all but newest, mirroring QA-021) + loose out/preprocess.*.xml/out/*.log; README documents test-scratch size + that clean-runs reclaims it (or the targeted rm -rf out/test-scratch that preserves the cache). A test asserts every top-level out/ name a canonical run produces is prunable or excluded-with-reason.
findings.fix_note: FIXED: clean-runs.sh now also reclaims out/test-scratch (the largest consumer) — all but the newest token dir — plus loose out/preprocess.*.xml / out/*.log, while preserving out/cache and the newest token; README 'Disk usage' documents test-scratch size + that clean-runs reclaims it (the cache-preserving alternative to rm -rf out). New test MaUxR21_CleanRuns_ReclaimsTestScratch_AndLooseFiles_PreservesCacheAndNewest plants stale token dirs + loose files under an isolated --out-dir and asserts reclaim + newest/cache preserved.

findings.description: MA-VI-1's malformed-report consistency guarantee is class-incomplete: the schema does not constrain per_probe_results[].probe to manifest probe IDs, so a schema-valid child report with an unknown/superset composite key (e.g. P99(A)=pass) trips the same inconsistency MA-VI-1 named - verdict = INCOMPLETE(malformed-report) (fail-closed via ComputeRouteVerdict unknown-key check) while per_probe_results shows all-pass and exit_report_matrix=consistent (ToKeyedResults doesn't throw on non-duplicates). BOUNDED: no COMPATIBLE, exit stays fail-closed at 20 via the QA-016 clause. Report-honesty, not a breach. Pre-existing in the old TryAdd view, so not newly opened - but the fix is class-incomplete against its own stated class.
findings.instance_fix: On a ToKeyedResults/schema-clean report, also detect unknown/superset composite keys vs the manifest instantiation at ingestion and set malformedRoutes[route] (same path duplicates take), so per-probe view + exit_report_matrix agree with the malformed verdict; OR constrain per_probe_results[].probe to the manifest probe-id set in the schema. Induced test per malformed trigger (duplicate/unknown/superset) asserting all three surfaces agree.
findings.fix_note: FIXED (the one production C# change): the aggregator now detects unknown/SUPERSET composite keys vs the manifest instantiation AT INGESTION (mirroring ComputeRouteVerdict's exact-equality unknown-key leg) and takes the SAME malformed path duplicates take, so the verdict, the per_probe_results view, AND the exit_report_matrix all read malformed — not just the verdict. Launch-through-binary test MaViR21_UnknownSupersetKey_MalformedOnAllThreeSurfaces feeds a schema-clean route report with P99(B) and asserts all three surfaces agree + fail-closed exit; on reversion the verdict stays malformed but the per-probe view is all-pass and the matrix consistent. Validated directly against the built aggregator.

findings.description: The suite-receipt fix is behaviorally tested on the CONSUME side (aggregator launched with fabricated receipts) but has NO committed test on the PRODUCE side: no test reads the substrate run's receipts/suite-receipt.json and asserts its {run_id,nonce,suite_exit,source_manifest_sha256} binding. Also the receipt PATH is a duplicated shell literal (run-spike.sh) vs C# const (RunLayout.SuiteReceiptRelativePath) with no binding test (MA-XC-7's general shell<->C# constant sweep was only done for evidence_schema_version). A regression breaking emission (wrong nonce/path/omission) leaves all aggregator tests green and surfaces only as a canonical run silently downgrading to unknown.
findings.instance_fix: Add a test reading RunContext.RunRoot()/receipts/suite-receipt.json from the substrate run, asserting run_id/nonce == run context, suite_exit==0, and the path equals RunLayout.SuiteReceiptRelativePath (mirror the QA013/Ea008 shell<->C# binding pattern).
findings.fix_note: FIXED (produce side + shell<->C# binding): new test MaIdR22_SuiteReceipt_ProduceSideBinding_AndPathMatchesConstant asserts (1) run-spike.sh emits the receipt at $RUN_ROOT/receipts/suite-receipt.json whose relative form == RunLayout.SuiteReceiptRelativePath (the previously-unbound duplicated literal), and (2) a completed canonical run's suite receipt binds to its own run-context — run_id matches, nonce present, suite_exit==0, source_manifest_sha256 == run-context source_set_sha256. The CURRENT run's receipt is written only after the suite (unreadable then); the out/current referent (protected from prune by MA-RB-R2-4) is the readable substrate.

findings.description: The canonical-start auto-prune prune_run_roots (run-spike.sh) is a SEPARATE shell implementation from clean-runs.sh, and only clean-runs.sh has a behavioral test. The class_fix's 'N consecutive runs leave a bounded root count' was not built; the two keep-N implementations can drift and a prune_run_roots regression (the HIGH unbounded-disk concern) stays green.
findings.instance_fix: Factor the keep-N logic into one shell function both entry points call, OR add a test that plants runid-* roots and drives prune_run_roots directly asserting bounded count + live-root protection.
findings.fix_note: FIXED (test drives the real prune_run_roots; factoring deferred by design). Rather than fold two hardened scripts into a shared sourced lib (which complicates the per-script allowlist/hardening static scans), the OR branch was taken: --phase prune-run-roots drives the REAL prune_run_roots (the same function the outer watchdog calls). New test MaIdR23_Prune_DrivesRealPruneRunRoots_LeavesBoundedCount plants 8 roots, keeps 3, asserts exactly the newest 3 survive; MaRbR21/MaRbR24 drive it for live-root + referent protection. clean-runs.sh keeps its own impl but now shares the same referent-protection + keep-N SEMANTICS, each with its own behavioral test.

findings.description: The provision/fetch runtime env-construction is source-scan-only (Pr006 test greps require_env_key; Ea008 tests the profile FILE shape). Only the harness class gets a runtime 'child actually received this env' assertion. The two new network launch classes never get a behavioral received-env (decoy-first PATH, run-root TMPDIR) assertion. Fail-closed, bounded, but the runtime enforcement is scan-only.
findings.instance_fix: Extend the child-received-env behavioral assertion (or an env-audit receipt check) to a provision/fetch launch.
findings.fix_note: FIXED (received-env, not scan): new test MaIdR24_ProvisionAndFetch_ReceivedDecoyFirstPath_RunRootTmpdir reads the SUBSTRATE run's env-audit receipt (the exact dictionary passed at each launch) and asserts the provision AND fetch launches actually received a decoy-first PATH (<run-root>/decoys...) and a run-root TMPDIR (<run-root>/tmp). On reversion (either class stops going through the constructed per-launch environment) its entry is missing or its PATH/TMPDIR wrong.

findings.description: The MA-UX-5/RB-2 staged seed leaks out/cache/packages.staged.$$ (hundreds of MB) on a deadline-kill/crash mid-seed, and NOTHING reaps it: on a seed-phase timeout phase_guard cache -> fail_run exits BEFORE the else-branch rm -rf CACHE_STAGED; a SIGKILL between mkdir and swap leaks it too. No glob cleans packages.staged.*/packages.old anywhere, and clean-runs/prune never touch out/cache. PID-suffixed names -> each killed seed leaks a fresh copy -> unbounded growth in the one dir no tool prunes.
findings.instance_fix: trap-clean CACHE_STAGED on the seed path (or move rm -rf CACHE_STAGED before phase_guard), and glob-reap out/cache/packages.staged.*/packages.old at controller start.
findings.fix_note: FIXED: the seed drops packages.staged.$$ on ANY non-success BEFORE phase_guard's fail_run/exit (was AFTER), and reap_cache_staging runs at controller start to sweep leaked packages.staged.<dead-pid> / packages.<dead-pid>.old / legacy packages.old — a LIVE seeder's staged dir (pid alive) is never clobbered. New test MaRbR22_ReapCacheStaging_ReapsDead_PreservesLive (via --phase reap-cache-staging) proves dead-pid staging reaped, live-pid staging preserved (int.MaxValue is a never-live pid).

findings.description: The cache seed's 'concurrent cold-cache race can't corrupt it' claim is not met. Two concurrent cold runs both pass [ ! -f MARKER ] and both seed (no lock). Publish uses shared name packages.old + plain mv (no -T): in the interleave where both skip move-to-old, run1 mv staged1->packages, then run2 mv staged2->packages (now a dir) moves staged2 INSIDE it as packages/packages.staged.<pid2>; marker then written -> a doubled/nested cache marked COMPLETE, never re-seeded, tar-copied into every run root (cross-run disk amplification + the 'torn cache marked complete' state MA-UX-5/RB-2 claimed impossible).
findings.instance_fix: Publish with mv -T/--no-target-directory (or rename-into-place only when target absent under a lock), PID-unique .old names, and guard the whole seed with the out/-level lock from MA-RB-R2-1. Two-concurrent-cold-seeders induced test asserting exactly one clean copy.
findings.fix_note: FIXED: publish_cache_staged serializes the whole publish under the out/-level lock, RE-CHECKS the marker after acquiring (a concurrent cold seeder that already won -> drop our staged copy), rotates any existing dir to a PID-UNIQUE .old, and swaps with mv -T/--no-target-directory so a staged dir can never be moved INSIDE an existing packages/ (the nested/doubled-cache-marked-complete state). New test MaRbR23_ConcurrentColdSeed_OneCleanCopy_NeverNested (deterministic interleave simulation, --phase cache-publish): (a) marker-present -> winner's copy untouched, staged dropped; (b) torn partial dir -> mv -T replaces it wholesale, never nests. On reversion (plain mv / no re-check) case (b) nests.

findings.description: Both the prune comment (run-spike.sh 'NEVER the out/current pointer target') and clean-runs.sh ('NEVER touches out/current', message 'out/current ... untouched') assert the out/current pointer's TARGET is protected - but NO code reads out/current to exclude its target. They skip only the out/current DIRECTORY name. out/current points at the previous canonical run's runid-* root, buried below that run's own newer nested --out roots; with ~3 nested roots, clean-runs.sh at DEFAULT --keep 3 keeps the 3 nested and deletes the 4th-newest = the pointer's target -> out/current dangles, and the tool prints 'out/current ... untouched'. Newly reachable (no prune existed before round 1). Fails closed downstream (RunContext.RequirePath, TA-B13) but silently breaks the documented dev-loop pointer the fix promised to protect.
findings.instance_fix: Read out/current/spike.runsettings's SPIKE_RUN_CONTEXT, resolve its run root, add it to the protect set in BOTH prune_run_roots and clean-runs.sh; reject --keep 0; correct the 'untouched' wording to mean the directory only. AP-004: a 'NEVER deletes X' guarantee must be backed by an enforcement line the reverting test would break.
findings.fix_note: FIXED (both prune entry points): prune_run_roots and clean-runs.sh read out/current/spike.runsettings's SPIKE_RUN_CONTEXT, resolve the run root it TARGETS, and add it to the protect set; --keep 0 is refused in both. The 'untouched' wording now means the directories AND the pointer's target, backed by the skip. New test MaRbR24_Prune_ProtectsOutCurrentReferent_AndRejectsKeepZero plants the referent as OLDEST and asserts it survives keep-1 (deleted on reversion) + --keep 0 exits 20. clean-runs referent protection covered by MaUxR21 (isolated out-dir).

findings.description: The keep-newest-5 auto-prune fires at the start of EVERY canonical run and rm -rf's every out/runid-* beyond newest 5 with ZERO runtime output. An existing user upgrading to 81f2892 with ~100 roots (~100GB) has them silently deleted on their FIRST post-upgrade run before it starts. Documented in README but nothing prints at runtime, while the sibling clean-runs.sh DOES announce. Not load-bearing loss (gitignored/regenerable; out/current+out/cache safe) but a user who archived a run root by path loses it silently; also O(n^2) -nt sweep + ~100 rm -rf on first run.
findings.instance_fix: Have prune_run_roots emit one stderr line naming the count removed + keep-count (mirror clean-runs.sh), so the first-post-upgrade mass deletion is visible. Any new automatic destructive default announces what it removed + is announced on first activation against a pre-upgrade substrate.
findings.fix_note: FIXED (folded into the prune fix): prune_run_roots emits one stderr line naming the count removed + keep-count (mirrors clean-runs.sh) — a first-post-upgrade mass deletion is now visible at runtime. Observed in the canonical run ('pruned 1 old run root(s) ... keeping the newest 5') and asserted by MaRbR21_Prune_SkipsLiveRunRoot_AndAnnouncesCount (Assert.Contains 'pruned' on stderr).

findings.description: The MA-UC-4 fix bound the project-enforcement perimeter across 3 copies (sln <-> SpikePaths.Projects <-> run-spike.sh lock-receipt list) but left a 4th unbound: the aggregator's SpikeProjects() (Program.cs), which drives the per-project lockDigests in run-report binding. Consistent today, but on a project addition the sln<->SpikePaths test goes red while SpikeProjects() silently omits the new project's lock digest from evidence - a fail-open narrowing of INV-001 lock-pin binding no test catches.
findings.instance_fix: Extend the Uc4 test to assert the aggregator's SpikeProjects() name+path set == SpikePaths.Projects. The 'one authoritative source with equality tests against each copy' rule must enumerate ALL copies incl. aggregator-side.
findings.fix_note: Deferred to backlog per user disposition (round-2 LOW). Tracked as DF-004.

findings.description: env-profiles.json still declares SSL_CERT_FILE/SSL_CERT_DIR as <passthrough> for controller/provision/fetch/restore/build/test, but MA-HI-1's env -i strips SSL_CERT_* before the inner controller runs, so build_profile_env's passthrough branch always resolves empty. The launches never receive SSL_CERT_*. Not exploitable/fail-closed (fetches are SHA-256-pinned) but the profile advertises a passthrough the MA-HI-1 contract guarantees can never fire, plus a latent portability failure on minimal hosts.
findings.instance_fix: Drop the two dead <passthrough> keys from the affected classes, OR add SSL_CERT_FILE/DIR to the MA-HI-1 re-exec allowlist if passthrough is wanted. An env-i allowlist and the profile passthrough set must be reconciled by a static check.
findings.fix_note: Deferred to backlog per user disposition (round-2 LOW). Tracked as DF-005.

findings.description: The suite receipt carries source_manifest_sha256 (emitted by run-spike.sh, in the ContractTypes contract) but the aggregator reads only run_id/nonce/suite_exit - the field is never validated. Not a hole (run_id+nonce already bind the receipt to this run) but it READS like a source-identity binding while providing none (MA-VI-6's 'source-manifest digest binding' is carried but not enforced).
findings.instance_fix: Cross-check source_manifest_sha256 against run-context.json's source_set_sha256 so the receipt genuinely attests the source the suite validated, OR drop the field. A receipt field emitted but never consumed by its validator should be enforced or removed.
findings.fix_note: Deferred to backlog per user disposition (round-2 LOW). Tracked as DF-006.
findings.orchestrator_note: Merges the secondary observation in MA-XC-R2-2 (same write-only field).

findings.description: The MA-HI-3 (json_escape control chars) fix is locked only by a source-scan asserting the substitution strings \n/\t/\r appear in the function body. A semantic escaping bug that keeps those strings but mis-orders substitutions (a later backslash-escape double-escaping) would emit invalid JSON and stay green - a hostile-input finding where a cheap behavioral test was feasible.
findings.instance_fix: Feed a --run-root path (or report value) containing a raw newline through the real write_failed_report path and JsonDocument.Parse the emitted report. Hostile-input JSON-emission fixes get a round-trip (emit->parse) test, never a scan of the escaper body alone.
findings.fix_note: Deferred to backlog per user disposition (round-2 LOW). Tracked as DF-007.

findings.description: The aggregator's --nonce is optional (only --run-id required); every receipt check is 'receiptRunId != runId || (nonce.Length>0 && receiptNonce != nonce)', so omitting --nonce degrades all receipt binding (suite/restore/build) to run_id-only, and run_id is not secret (it appears in every on-disk report/receipt). Does not breach MA-VI-6's threat (presence of a suite receipt with suite_exit==0 is the real gate; forging needs run-root write access) but the class-fix intent 'receipt-bound to the run mint, never a bare argv claim' is only as strong as run_id unless --nonce is mandatory.
findings.instance_fix: Require a non-empty --nonce in the aggregator so binding degrades closed rather than silently to run_id.
findings.fix_note: Deferred to backlog per user disposition (round-2 LOW). Tracked as DF-008.

findings.description: The aggregator terminal summary (advertised in README as 'verdict, failed probes with reasons, report path - no JSON parsing needed') prints 'route A verdict: INCOMPLETE; failed probes: -' whenever all probes pass but final_suite_status != success (every variance run + the MA-VI-6 canonical downgrade) - StateText renders bare INCOMPLETE with an empty failed-probes list. A summary-only reader sees an unexplained INCOMPLETE with no failed probes; the reason is only in the JSON verdict_reason. MA-VI-6's downgrade adds a canonical path to reach it.
findings.instance_fix: Append the verdict reason to the summary line for non-probe-failure states, e.g. 'INCOMPLETE (suite-failure: final_suite_status=unknown)'. Any 'no JSON parsing needed' summary renders the reason for every non-COMPATIBLE state.
findings.fix_note: Deferred to backlog per user disposition (round-2 LOW). Tracked as DF-009.

findings.description: run-spike.sh/regen-sample.sh refuse an unhardened (no -p) invocation with exit 30; clean-runs.sh refuses the same class with exit 20; provision-z3.sh has NO -p self-check at all. Docs are consistent (no doc-vs-code contradiction) but a CI wrapper keying on '30=refused-unhardened vs 20=usage' misclassifies clean-runs' hardening refusal, and standalone provision-z3.sh runs unhardened silently.
findings.instance_fix: Make clean-runs use exit 30 for the unhardened refusal, add the same -p check + exit 30 to provision-z3.sh. One shared 'refuse unhardened -> exit 30' idiom across all four scripts, asserted by a static test.
findings.fix_note: Deferred to backlog per user disposition (round-2 LOW). Tracked as DF-010.

findings.description: The MA-ED-3 fix_note states the Ed3 test asserts 'no rooted host path (/home,/Users,/tmp,/opt,/var)', but the implemented assertion only checks four concrete strings (runId, 'run_directory', runRoot, SpikeRoot). A stable system-rooted path (/opt/dotnet, /usr/share/dotnet) leaking into a class-2 value would not be caught. Determinism impact nil (such paths are run-stable; class-2 path-bearing fields are canonicalized + framework/system assemblies excluded). The code comment is accurate; only the audit-artifact fix_note overstates.
findings.instance_fix: Align the MA-ED-3 fix_note wording to what Ed3 checks, OR add a generic rooted-path regex (^/(opt|var|usr|home|Users|tmp)/ or drive-letter) over the raw class-2 subtree. A 'no rooted path in class-2' assertion should test generically, not by enumerating known roots.
findings.fix_note: Deferred to backlog per user disposition (round-2 LOW). Tracked as DF-011.

findings.description: list_descendant_sessions lacks the sess==candidate leader-guard its sibling find_session_leader has: for candidate==ancestor it emits sid[candidate] unconditionally. Harmless today because setsid -w (no -f, job control off) doesn't fork so WRAPPER_PID is the inner session leader. Latent: if the setsid call ever forks (adding -f, set -m, or setsid already a group leader), sid[WRAPPER_PID] becomes the OUTER session, and on operator cancel the trap kills -TERM/-KILL -<parent-session> - signaling the outer watchdog's own group, potentially killing the run before the OperatorCancelled synthetic is written. UNCERTAIN (not triggerable in the current invocation contract).
findings.instance_fix: In list_descendant_sessions, continue when candidate==ancestor, or emit only when sid[candidate]==candidate (mirror find_session_leader). Session-sweep enumerators share one leader-identification helper.
findings.fix_note: Deferred to backlog per user disposition (round-2 LOW). Tracked as DF-012.
